633	    "position statement",
634	    "practice bulletin",
635	)
636	_GUIDELINE_YEAR_DOC_RE = re.compile(
637	    r"\b(?:19|20)\d{2}\b.{0,80}\bguidelines?\s+(?:for|on|update|focused update)\b"
638	)
639	# Primary-study / commentary titles that MENTION guidelines but are NOT
640	# guideline documents — never promoted (checked FIRST).
641	_GUIDELINE_TITLE_EXCLUSIONS = (
642	    "guideline-directed", "guideline directed",      # GDMT — a therapy, studied in primaries
643	    "guideline adherence", "guideline-adherent", "guideline adherent",
644	    "adherence to guideline", "adherence to the guideline",
645	    "guideline-concordant", "guideline concordant",
646	    "guideline implementation", "guideline-based", "guideline based",
647	    "guideline-recommended", "guideline recommended",
648	    "non-guideline", "off-guideline",
649	)
650	# iter-5 (Codex P1): study-ABOUT-a-document framings, anchored to the TITLE
651	# START. A primary study that validates / uses / evaluates a consensus statement,
652	# decision pathway, or guideline is NOT itself the issued document — e.g.
653	# "Validation of the 2019 Expert Consensus Algorithm ..." / "Validation of the
654	# ACC Expert Consensus Decision Pathway ...". Issued guideline/consensus
655	# documents START with a year or a society name (or "Clinical Practice
656	# Guideline" / "Expert Consensus ..."), NEVER with a study verb. Anchoring to
657	# the start (not substring-anywhere) avoids over-excluding real guidelines whose
658	# scope phrase contains a verb mid-title (e.g. "Guideline for the Evaluation of
659	# Chest Pain").
660	_STUDY_FRAMING_TITLE_PREFIXES = (
661	    "validation of", "validating", "a validation",
662	    "use of", "using", "utilization of", "utility of",
663	    "impact of", "implementation of", "application of",
664	    "evaluation of", "comparison of", "comparing",
665	    "association of", "association between", "predictors of",
666	    "outcomes of", "outcomes after", "effect of", "effects of",
667	    "efficacy and safety of", "efficacy of", "safety of",
668	    "adherence to", "real-world",
669	)
670	
671	
672	def _title_signals_clinical_guideline(title: str) -> bool:
673	    """I-bug-771 (#812): detect an ISSUED clinical-practice-guideline DOCUMENT
674	    title for the guideline-authority promotion in Rule 8c. Exclusions checked
675	    FIRST (GDMT / adherence / implementation primaries). Then a year-anchored
676	    "guideline(s) for|on" document pattern OR an explicit document-type statement
677	    (consensus / scientific / position statement / practice bulletin). Rejects
678	    undated guideline-comparison commentary. Narrower than
679	    _detect_guideline_or_explainer_title (which fires on explainer/policy titles
680	    that must stay T4)."""
681	    if not title:
682	        return False
683	    t = title.lower()
684	    if any(x in t for x in _GUIDELINE_TITLE_EXCLUSIONS):
685	        return False
686	    # iter-5: a study ABOUT a document (validation/use/impact/...) starts with a
687	    # study verb; issued documents do not. Reject these before the document
688	    # markers so "Validation of the ... Expert Consensus ..." is not promoted.
689	    t_start = t.lstrip("\"'([ ")
690	    if any(t_start.startswith(p) for p in _STUDY_FRAMING_TITLE_PREFIXES):
691	        return False
692	    if any(m in t for m in _GUIDELINE_DOC_STATEMENT_MARKERS):
693	        return True
694	    return bool(_GUIDELINE_YEAR_DOC_RE.search(t))
695	
696	# M-18a (DR audit pass 1): when the fetcher records the URL as
697	# doi.org/<prefix>/<suffix>, the classifier cannot know from the
698	# domain alone that this is a peer-reviewed journal. These DOI
699	# prefixes are registered to specific peer-reviewed publishers and
700	# can be trusted as equivalent to PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS
701	# membership. Source: Crossref registrant records.
702	PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES = frozenset({
703	    "10.1056",  # NEJM
704	    "10.1001",  # JAMA Network
705	    "10.1016",  # Elsevier (Lancet, Cell, Cell Metabolism, etc.)
706	    "10.1038",  # Nature Publishing Group
707	    "10.1126",  # Science / AAAS
708	    "10.1136",  # BMJ
709	    "10.1002",  # Wiley
710	    "10.1007",  # Springer
711	    "10.1111",  # Wiley
712	    "10.1210",  # Endocrine Society
713	    "10.2337",  # American Diabetes Association
714	    "10.1093",  # Oxford University Press
715	    "10.1161",  # American Heart Association
716	    "10.1152",  # American Physiological Society
717	    "10.4239",  # World Journal of Diabetes
718	    "10.1172",  # J Clin Invest
719	    "10.1059",  # Am Coll Physicians / ACP
720	    "10.3389",  # Frontiers
721	    "10.3390",  # MDPI
722	    "10.1371",  # PLOS
723	    "10.7759",  # Cureus
724	    "10.1080",  # Taylor & Francis
725	    "10.1089",  # Mary Ann Liebert
726	    "10.1155",  # Hindawi (peer-reviewed but mixed quality)
727	})
728	
729	
730	def _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(url: str) -> bool:
731	    """Return True if URL points at a DOI with a peer-reviewed
732	    publisher prefix (M-18a DR audit fix)."""
733	    if not url:
734	        return False
735	    u = url.lower()
736	    if "doi.org/" not in u:
737	        return False
738	    # Extract DOI after doi.org/
739	    try:
740	        tail = u.split("doi.org/", 1)[1]
741	        prefix = tail.split("/", 1)[0]
742	        return prefix in PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES
743	    except (IndexError, ValueError):
744	        return False
745	
746	
747	def _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
748	    """F12 (GH #1245 / D12): True when a doi.org-hosted canonical DOI resolves
749	    to a real OpenAlex JOURNAL venue.
750	
751	    THE BUG (run-killer): a canonical-DOI journal hosted on doi.org (e.g. JEP
752	    10.1257, JPE 10.1086) is NOT on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (host is
753	    `doi.org`, not the publisher), and its DOI prefix may not be in the
754	    hard-coded PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES allowlist. R9's unverified-host guard
755	    then demotes it to T4. ~50 such sources fell to T4 on the workforce corpus,
756	    collapsing the resolved-venue tier and false-firing abort_corpus_inadequate.
757	
758	    THE FIX: instead of whack-a-mole prefix expansion, TRUST the resolved
759	    OpenAlex venue. A doi.org host with OpenAlex `source_type == "journal"` AND
760	    a non-empty venue name IS a peer-reviewed journal — count it as such rather
761	    than defaulting to the doi.org-host prior. This widens R9's unverified-host
762	    EXEMPTION only; it never relaxes a faithfulness gate and is scoped strictly
763	    to doi.org-hosted canonical DOIs so the BUG-M-11 trade-content guard on
764	    non-DOI hosts is untouched. SR/MA, narrative, guideline, conference-abstract
765	    and low-quality-OA branches all run BEFORE / AFTER this exemption and still
766	    win (e.g. a doi.org/10.3390 MDPI work still hits _is_low_quality_oa -> T4).
767	
768	    Scope is enforced on the PARSED HOST (host == doi.org or *.doi.org, e.g.
769	    dx.doi.org), NOT a substring of the URL — a non-doi.org trade host that
770	    embeds `doi.org/` in its path or query (e.g.
771	    `https://trade.example/redirect/https://doi.org/10.x`) must NOT pass, so the
772	    BUG-M-11 trade-content guard stays intact (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1).
773	    """
774	    host = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
775	    if not (host == "doi.org" or host.endswith(".doi.org")):
776	        return False
777	    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
778	    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
779	    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)
780	
781	
782	def _is_known_scholarly_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
783	    """I-arch-011 B17/B11 (KEYSTONE): True when the source is a recognized
784	    scholarly VENUE on venue/identifier evidence ALONE — independent of how
785	    much body text the fetcher happened to return.
786	
787	    This is the shared detector that drives the "do NOT demote a real journal
788	    to the T7 length floor" carve-out on BOTH classifier paths (the legacy
789	    rules path R1 and the authority-model path). A source qualifies when ANY
790	    of these venue signals is present:
791	      * the host is on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (NEJM / Lancet / BMJ /
792	        Nature / Brain via academic.oup.com / Movement Disorders via
793	        onlinelibrary.wiley.com, ...) — parent-domain match;
794	      * the URL is a doi.org canonical DOI whose registrant prefix is a known
795	        peer-reviewed publisher (_has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix);
796	      * the URL is a doi.org canonical DOI that resolved to a real OpenAlex
797	        JOURNAL venue (_is_doi_org_journal_with_venue);
798	      * OpenAlex independently resolved the work to a JOURNAL source_type with
799	        a non-empty venue name.
800	
801	    It is a VENUE-AUTHORITY signal, NOT a grounding/adequacy signal: a known
802	    venue keeps its authority weight even when the fetch is an empty stub, but
803	    the caller MUST set fetch_degraded=True so the stub is excluded from
804	    grounded-content adequacy downstream (no laundering). Adds no cap/floor —
805	    it only RESTORES the venue weighting the length-floor was clobbering.
806	    """
807	    domain = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
808	    if _domain_matches(domain, PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS):
809	        return True
810	    if _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(signals.url):
811	        return True
812	    if _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals):
813	        return True
814	    # I-deepfix-001 (U10 tier_venue_exemption, #1344): two gold venue-authority
815	    # signals ALREADY on the classifier input were ignored, so a real peer-reviewed
816	    # journal fetched from a BARE-PDF URL (no peer-reviewed venue domain, no doi.org
817	    # host, no OpenAlex journal source_type stamped on the PDF row) was over-demoted
818	    # to the T7/UNKNOWN length floor. Both signals are ones a signal-less, merely
819	    # scholarly-TITLED scam page cannot present: OpenAlex independently resolved the
820	    # work as peer-reviewed, OR a resolved canonical DOI (registrant prefix "10.").
821	    # WEIGHT-not-drop (§-1.3): only RESTORES the venue weight the length-floor was
822	    # clobbering — the caller still sets fetch_degraded=True so the stub is excluded
823	    # from grounded-content adequacy (no laundering). No cap/floor; engine untouched.
824	    if signals.openalex_is_peer_reviewed is True:
825	        return True
826	    if (signals.doi or "").strip().lower().startswith("10."):
827	        return True
828	    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
829	    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
830	    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)
831	
832	
833	def _is_corroborated_scholarly_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
834	    """I-wire-001 (Codex iter-2 P1): STRICTER venue-only predicate for the B2 LLM-tiering
835	    venue-corroboration cap ONLY. It is deliberately NARROWER than ``_is_known_scholarly_venue``:
836	    it requires a genuinely RECOGNIZED peer-reviewed VENUE and EXCLUDES both a bare ``doi`` "10."
837	    prefix and a lone ``openalex_is_peer_reviewed`` flag.
838	
839	    Why split from ``_is_known_scholarly_venue``: the U10 length-floor widening added a bare-DOI
840	    ("10.") and a lone peer-reviewed-flag branch so a real journal fetched from a BARE-PDF URL
841	    keeps its venue WEIGHT at the T7 length floor (a WEIGHT restoration, §-1.3). But that same
842	    helper is ALSO the B2 corroboration predicate whose entire job is to CAP an uncorroborated
843	    top-tier (T1/T2) LLM verdict that rests on nothing but a bare DOI + a scholarly-sounding title
844	    (the off-topic Russian-cosmetics ev_061 mis-tiered T1/0.95 was exactly this). Reusing the
845	    widened helper there re-opened the cap: a bare-DOI-only row would satisfy corroboration and
846	    slip its T1/T2 verdict past the cap. So the B2 path uses THIS strict predicate — corroboration
847	    requires an actual venue signal a signal-less, merely scholarly-TITLED page cannot present:
848	      * host on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (parent-domain match);
849	      * a doi.org canonical DOI whose registrant prefix is a known peer-reviewed publisher;
850	      * a doi.org canonical DOI resolved to a real OpenAlex JOURNAL venue;
851	      * OpenAlex resolved the work to a JOURNAL source_type WITH a non-empty venue name.
852	    A bare ``doi`` "10." and a lone ``openalex_is_peer_reviewed`` are NOT venues, so they do NOT
853	    corroborate. WEIGHT-only (§-1.3): the cap this predicate guards can ONLY lower an
854	    uncorroborated top tier to the deterministic rules-floor — it never raises a tier, never drops
855	    a source, never gates release. The faithfulness engine is untouched."""
856	    domain = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
857	    if _domain_matches(domain, PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS):
858	        return True
859	    if _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(signals.url):
860	        return True
861	    if _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals):
862	        return True
863	    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
864	    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
865	    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)
866	
867	
868	def _is_low_quality_oa(domain: str, url: str) -> bool:
869	    """I-bug-771 (#812): True if the source is a low-quality / high-volume OA
870	    publisher (MDPI) by domain OR by URL-embedded DOI prefix. Used to deny T1
871	    primary credit in R9/R10 (SR/MA still routes to T2 in the earlier branch)."""
872	    if _domain_matches(domain, LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOMAINS):
873	        return True
874	    u = (url or "").lower()
875	    # Exact DOI-prefix match (iter-2 Codex P2: avoid substring false-positives
876	    # such as 10.33901 matching 10.3390). Extract the registrant prefix after
877	    # doi.org/ exactly, mirroring _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix.
878	    if "doi.org/" in u:
879	        try:
880	            prefix = u.split("doi.org/", 1)[1].split("/", 1)[0]
881	            if prefix in LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOI_PREFIXES:
882	                return True
883	        except (IndexError, ValueError):
884	            pass
885	    # DOI embedded in a publisher path (e.g. /10.3390/...): require both
886	    # boundary slashes so 10.33901 cannot match 10.3390.
887	    return any(f"/{p}/" in u for p in LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOI_PREFIXES)
888	
889	
890	# Preprint servers (T4/T5 candidates — not peer-reviewed; caller may
891	# tier-down further based on funding disclosure).
892	PREPRINT_DOMAINS = frozenset({
893	    "arxiv.org", "biorxiv.org", "medrxiv.org", "ssrn.com",
894	    "preprints.org", "researchsquare.com", "papers.ssrn.com",
895	    "osf.io",
896	})
897	
898	# Student-journal / low-credibility-journal patterns. Plan A-S3-A note
899	# flagged nhsjs.com (National High School Journal of Science) as
900	# inappropriately tagged GOLD in PG_LB_SA_02. These are not predatory
901	# per se but don't meet T1/T2 peer-review standards.
902	STUDENT_JOURNAL_DOMAINS = frozenset({
903	    "nhsjs.com",
904	})
905	
906	# Abstract-only / stub-content domains where typical content is just a
907	# title + abstract. Used as a hint; falls through to content-length check.
908	ABSTRACT_ONLY_DOMAINS = frozenset({
909	    "semanticscholar.org",
910	    "openalex.org",
911	})
912	
913	# Content-length thresholds (chars of fetched body).
914	MIN_T1_T4_CONTENT_CHARS = 3000  # below this, downgrade to T7 stub
915	T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS = 1000    # below this, always T7 stub
916	
917	
918	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
919	# Helpers
920	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
921	
922	def _normalize_domain(url: str) -> str:
923	    """Extract lowercase domain from URL, stripping www. and path."""
924	    if not url:
925	        return ""
926	    try:
927	        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}")
928	        host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
929	        return host[4:] if host.startswith("www.") else host
930	    except Exception:
931	        return ""
932	
933	
934	def _domain_matches(domain: str, domain_set: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
935	    """Check whether domain (or any parent) is in the set."""
936	    if not domain:
937	        return False
938	    if domain in domain_set:
939	        return True
940	    # Parent-domain match: foo.fda.gov matches fda.gov
941	    parts = domain.split(".")
942	    for i in range(len(parts)):
943	        parent = ".".join(parts[i:])
944	        if parent in domain_set:
945	            return True
946	    return False
947	
948	
949	def _detect_systematic_review_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
950	    """Heuristic: title contains 'systematic review' / 'meta-analysis'."""
951	    if not title:
952	        return False
953	    t = title.lower()
954	    return any(k in t for k in (
955	        "systematic review", "meta-analysis", "meta analysis",
956	        "network meta-analysis", "cochrane review", "umbrella review",
957	        "scoping review",
958	    ))
959	
960	
961	def _detect_narrative_review_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
962	    """Heuristic: title contains 'review' but not systematic / meta."""
963	    if not title:
964	        return False
965	    t = title.lower()
966	    if "review" in t and not _detect_systematic_review_from_title(title):
967	        return True
968	    # Commentary / editorial / perspective markers
969	    return any(k in t for k in (
970	        "commentary", "editorial", "perspective", "opinion",
971	        "viewpoint",
972	    ))
973	
974	
975	def _detect_conference_abstract(title: str, url: str = "") -> bool:
976	    """Heuristic: title / URL signals a conference abstract.
977	
978	    Matches:
979	    - explicit keywords (conference abstract, poster, oral presentation)
980	    - "1745-P:" / "82-OR:" / "P-123:" abstract-number prefixes commonly
981	      used by ADA, ENDO, ESC, etc.
982	    - Pass-16 (Codex full-scale pass 1): Endocrine Society / ENDO
983	      day-prefixed presentation IDs like "THU296" / "MON-123" / "FRI42"
984	      / "SAT-56" / "SUN-78" that prefix supplement abstracts.
985	    - URLs containing "/Supplement_" or "/abstract/" or journal-issue
986	      supplement paths, or "/jes/article-pdf/.../Supplement_"
987	    """
988	    if title:
989	        t = title.lower()
990	        if any(k in t for k in (
991	            "conference abstract", "poster", "oral presentation",
992	            "abstract p", "abstract po",
993	        )):
994	            return True
995	        # Numbered abstract prefix (e.g., "1745-P: Long-Term Safety...")
996	        if re.match(r"^\s*\d+-[A-Z]+:", title):
997	            return True
998	        # Numbered "P-" prefix
999	        if re.match(r"^\s*P-\d+", title):
1000	            return True
1001	        # Pass-16 (Codex full-scale pass 1): Endocrine Society day-letter
1002	        # presentation IDs. Pattern: MON/TUE/WED/THU/FRI/SAT/SUN + 2-4
1003	        # digits, optionally with hyphen. Must be at title start.
1004	        if re.match(
1005	            r"^\s*(MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI|SAT|SUN)-?\d{2,4}\b",
1006	            title, re.IGNORECASE,
1007	        ):
1008	            return True
1009	        # Pass-16: abstract-ID-like prefixes commonly seen on JES/JCEM
1010	        # supplements, e.g., "OR01-2", "OR30-04", "SUN-245"
1011	        if re.match(r"^\s*OR\d+-\d+\b", title, re.IGNORECASE):
1012	            return True
1013	    if url:
1014	        u = url.lower()
1015	        if "/supplement_" in u or "/supplement/" in u:
1016	            return True
1017	        # Pass-16: "/jes/article-pdf/.../Supplement_1/..." pattern
1018	        if "/article-pdf/" in u and "supplement" in u:
1019	            return True
1020	    return False
1021	
1022	
1023	# Narrative-review / commentary-flavored keywords. Broader than the
1024	# "systematic review" family. Surfaces commentary-ish articles in
1025	# peer-reviewed venues (J Obes Metab Syndr, Postgraduate Med, etc.).
1026	# M-18a (DR audit pass 1): STRONG narrative markers — always fire
1027	# regardless of primary-study signals. These phrases unambiguously
1028	# indicate that the paper is NOT a primary RCT even if it references
1029	# one (e.g., post-hoc analyses of a SURPASS trial ARE narrative).
1030	_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_STRONG_MARKERS = (
1031	    # Case reports / series — always narrative regardless of primary terms
1032	    "case report", "case reports", "case study",
1033	    "a case of", "a case report",
1034	    # Secondary / post-hoc analyses — always narrative; the primary
1035	    # paper is the parent RCT, not the post-hoc report
1036	    "post hoc", "post-hoc", "post hoc analysis", "post-hoc analysis",
1037	    "secondary analysis",
1038	    "pooled analysis",
1039	    "subgroup analysis",
1040	    # Program-level pooled reporting — still narrative
1041	    "in the step program", "in the step programme",
1042	    "in the surpass program", "in the surmount program",
1043	    "in the rewind program", "in the leader program",
1044	    "in the sustain program", "in the pioneer program",
1045	    "in the select program",
1046	    # M-18c (Codex pass 10 advisory): narrative framings about
1047	    # randomized trials are reviews ABOUT RCTs, not RCTs themselves.
1048	    # These must fire even when the title also contains "randomized".
1049	    "beyond randomized", "beyond rcts",
1050	    "update on randomized", "update on rcts",
1051	    "the role of randomized", "role of randomized",
1052	    "interpreting randomized", "interpreting rcts",
1053	    "overview of randomized", "overview of rcts",
1054	)
1055	
1056	# M-18a: WEAK narrative markers — defer to primary-study signals when
1057	# present. These phrases can appear in both narrative reviews AND in
1058	# primary RCT titles (e.g., NEJM "X as Compared with Y for the
1059	# Treatment of Z" uses "for the treatment of" as a study population
1060	# delimiter, not as a narrative frame).
1061	_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_WEAK_MARKERS = (
1062	    "a game changer", "game-changer", "game changer",
1063	    "update on ", "the role of ",
1064	    "overview of ", "advances in ",
1065	    "a review of ",
1066	    "the upcoming", "the coming", "on the horizon",
1067	    "perspectives on", "viewpoints on",
1068	    "against obesity", "for obesity",
1069	    "for the treatment of", "for the management of",
1070	    "perspective for", "perspective on ", "a perspective",
1071	    "perspectives for",
1072	    "primary care providers", "primary care physician",
1073	    "for clinicians", "for physicians",
1074	    "prescribing ",
1075	    "what the clinician", "what clinicians",
1076	)
1077	
1078	# Preserve the combined tuple for backwards compatibility with any
1079	# downstream code that imports _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_KEYWORDS directly.
1080	_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_KEYWORDS = (
1081	    _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_STRONG_MARKERS + _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_WEAK_MARKERS
1082	)
1083	
1084	
1085	def _detect_narrative_flavor_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
1086	    """Stronger-than-generic detector for narrative / commentary flavor.
1087	
1088	    M-18a (DR audit pass 1): split narrative markers into STRONG (case
1089	    report, post-hoc analysis, pooled analysis, program-level) and
1090	    WEAK (for the treatment of, update on, perspective for). Strong
1091	    markers always fire. Weak markers defer to primary-study signals
1092	    so NEJM head-to-head RCTs like "Tirzepatide as Compared with
1093	    Semaglutide for the Treatment of Obesity" are not demoted.
1094	    """

file.content:
"""
Tier classifier for retrieved sources — T1 through T7 + UNKNOWN.

Part of HONEST-REBUILD Phase 2 (plan:
C:/Users/msn/.claude/plans/lovely-finding-firefly.md).

Classifies every retrieved URL at fetch time into a tier so the pipeline
can surface the corpus quality distribution to the user BEFORE composition
starts. Fixes the defects documented in PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT.md
Section E-01:
    - Patch D over-assigned GOLD at ~24% error rate
    - Novo HCP portals tagged GOLD (should be BRONZE/industry)
    - News and student journals tagged GOLD
    - Regulatory documents (CDA-AMC) tagged UNKNOWN when they should be GOLD
    - No-match defaulted to BRONZE silently

Key discipline:
    1. UNKNOWN on no-match (NOT silent BRONZE) — surfaces misconfiguration
       rather than hiding it. The plan A-F04 "UNKNOWN ambiguity" gets
       resolved by BRONZE vs UNKNOWN being meaningfully different:
       UNKNOWN = "rules could not decide; requires user review";
       BRONZE = "rules positively identified low-tier source".
    2. Rules fire in priority order; first match wins.
    3. Each ClassificationResult carries the reasons list so the user
       can audit the decision (and so later reshapes can pin regressions
       to specific rule matches).
    4. No LLM call in the classifier — pure rules. An LLM-in-the-loop
       variant may come later, but for Phase 2 a deterministic classifier
       is the right primitive.

Tier taxonomy:
    T1 — Peer-reviewed primary study (RCT, prospective cohort, case-control,
          cross-sectional with clear protocol, ClinicalTrials.gov with
          results, etc.)
    T2 — Peer-reviewed systematic review, meta-analysis, Cochrane review,
          network meta-analysis
    T3 — Government / regulatory body (FDA, EMA, NICE, CDA-AMC, WHO,
          MHRA, Health Canada, TGA, PMDA)
    T4 — Peer-reviewed narrative review, commentary, editorial, perspective
    T5 — Industry-funded report (pharmaceutical company HCP portal, drug
          monograph from manufacturer, industry advocacy with funding
          disclosure)
    T6 — News / blog / non-peer-reviewed web content (mainstream news,
          industry press release distribution, commentary blog)
    T7 — Abstract-only / conference abstract / Semantic Scholar stub
          (content < 1000 chars) / paper behind paywall where only title
          + abstract were retrieved
    UNKNOWN — rules could not decide; user review required

Non-goals of this module:
    - Not a quality judgment of the CONTENT (that's the evaluator's job)
    - Not a hallucination detector (that's Vectara / our Phase 5 evaluator)
    - Not a plagiarism / novelty checker
    - Not a decision about whether to INCLUDE the source — only how to
      label it. The user (or auto-mode rules) decides inclusion.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class TierLevel(str, Enum):
    """Seven tiers plus UNKNOWN for surfacing classifier misconfiguration."""

    T1 = "T1"  # Peer-reviewed primary study
    T2 = "T2"  # Peer-reviewed SR / MA
    T3 = "T3"  # Government / regulatory
    T4 = "T4"  # Peer-reviewed narrative / commentary
    T5 = "T5"  # Industry-funded
    T6 = "T6"  # News / blog / non-peer-reviewed
    T7 = "T7"  # Abstract-only / stub
    UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"  # Rules could not decide — user review needed


@dataclass
class ClassificationSignals:
    """Input signals the classifier uses. All optional — partial signals OK."""

    url: str = ""
    fetched_content_length: int = 0  # chars of body fetched
    # OpenAlex-derived fields (when available)
    openalex_publication_type: str = ""  # "article", "preprint", "review", ...
    openalex_source_type: str = ""       # "journal", "repository", ...
    openalex_is_retracted: bool = False
    openalex_venue: str = ""             # journal or venue name
    openalex_is_peer_reviewed: bool | None = None  # None = unknown
    # I-ready-017 #1134: article DOI (normalized, no scheme). ADDITIVE — the
    # legacy rule body NEVER reads it; consumed only by the journal_only filter
    # (src/polaris_graph/nodes/journal_only_filter.py) on the ON path. Default
    # "" = byte-identical to HEAD.
    doi: str = ""
    # Bibliography-layer fields (when available)
    source_type_hint: str = ""  # upstream string like "industry_report"
    publisher: str = ""
    author_affiliations: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    funding_disclosures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # Free text for future keyword rules (e.g., title keywords)
    title: str = ""
    # BUG-M-17 (Codex pass 2): body-inspection secondary signal.
    # One of "SR_MA", "CASE_REPORT", "PERSPECTIVE", "GUIDELINE", or "".
    # Populated by live_retriever._detect_article_type_from_body when
    # fetched content contains article-type metadata or SR/MA/case-
    # report markers in the first 8KB. Used by classifier to override
    # title-only decisions when body evidence contradicts.
    body_article_type: str = ""
    # ── Phase 0a (GH #983) ADDITIVE fields — consumed ONLY by the authority
    # model when PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL=ON. The legacy rule body
    # (_classify_source_tier_rules) NEVER reads these, so OFF behaviour is
    # byte-identical to HEAD. `authority` is the C1 AuthoritySignals payload
    # (default None -> model degrades to LOW confidence, never fabricates).
    authority: "AuthoritySignals | None" = None
    fetched_body: str = ""          # optional structural body for junk detection
    structured_jsonld: str = ""     # optional extracted JSON-LD for junk/self-desc
    claim_vendor_token: str = ""    # optional claim-vendor token for self-interest


@dataclass
class ClassificationResult:
    """What the classifier returns. Always includes the reasoning trail."""

    tier: TierLevel
    confidence: float  # 0.0-1.0; 1.0 for deterministic rules, <1 for fuzzy
    reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    matched_rules: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    signals_used: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    # ── Phase 0a (GH #983) ADDITIVE fields. Default None on the OFF path
    # (byte-identical to HEAD for existing consumers). Populated ONLY by
    # _classify_via_authority_model when PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL=ON; emitted
    # but inert (no downstream gate reads them) in 0a — shadow only.
    authority_score: float | None = None
    source_class: str | None = None
    corroboration_count: int | None = None
    authority_confidence: str | None = None
    # ── I-arch-011 B17/B11 (KEYSTONE) ADDITIVE field. SEPARATES venue
    # authority from fetch completeness. True when a KNOWN SCHOLARLY VENUE
    # (Lancet / Brain / Movement Disorders, etc.) kept its venue-authority
    # tier even though the fetched body came back as a SHORT STUB (< the T7
    # stub threshold). The row is NOT demoted to the T7 length floor — it
    # keeps its real venue tier/weight (WEIGHT, never a drop) — but it is
    # LABELLED degraded so two SEPARATE downstream lanes can act on it:
    #   * a FETCH/re-fetch lane re-pulls the full text;
    #   * an ADEQUACY lane EXCLUDES degraded rows from grounded-content
    #     counts (so an empty stub's venue authority can never launder it
    #     into "adequate" grounded content — authority-weight and grounded-
    #     content-adequacy stay separate signals).
    # Default False = byte-identical to HEAD for every non-stub row.
    fetch_degraded: bool = False
    # ── I-deepfix-001 M2 ADDITIVE fields — per-citation document GENRE (orthogonal to the
    # T1-T7 credibility tier). Populated ONLY when PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT is ON (computed
    # at the ``classify_source_tier`` dispatcher so EVERY classifier path — rules,
    # authority-model, LLM-tiering — carries it); default None = byte-identical to HEAD.
    # ``document_type`` is a DocumentType.value; ``is_journal_article`` is True only for a
    # peer-reviewed journal/review article. WEIGHT-and-LABEL: no consumer reads these as a
    # drop/abort/release gate — they ride to the credibility-disclosure layer as advisory
    # genre, of the SAME class as ``authority_score``/``source_class``. The faithfulness
    # engine is untouched.
    document_type: str | None = None
    is_journal_article: bool | None = None

# practice-support paths are EXCLUDED here and stay T3 (Codex #812: "acc.org
# tools/dosing PDFs do not get T1/T2"). Content stubs are unaffected — Rule 1
# returns T7 before this rule, so a 297-char fetch can never be laundered up.
GUIDELINE_AUTHORITY_DOMAINS = frozenset({
    "escardio.org",      # European Society of Cardiology
    "nice.org.uk",       # NICE (UK)
    "ahajournals.org",   # AHA/ACC guidelines published in Circulation
    "jacc.org", "onlinejacc.org",
    "acc.org",           # ACC guideline pages (NOT /tools/ — excluded below)
})
_GUIDELINE_PATH_MARKERS = (
    "/guidelines/", "/guideline/", "/guidance/", "/recommendations/",
    "/scientific-documents/recom",  # ESC scientific-documents recommendations
)
# I-bug-771 (#812) iter-2 (Codex P1): canonical ACC/AHA guidelines are published
# as DOI ARTICLES (e.g. ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/CIR..., jacc.org/doi/...),
# NOT on a /guidelines/ path — so a path-only check misses them. On a recognized
# guideline-authority domain, these NARROW clinical-guideline title markers also
# promote to T2. Deliberately EXCLUDES the broad explainer/whitepaper/industry
# markers in _GUIDELINE_EXPLAINER_TITLE_MARKERS (those stay T4): a guideline is a
# practice/consensus/scientific statement, not a fact sheet or market insight.
# iter-4 (Codex P1+P2): distinguish an ISSUED guideline DOCUMENT from primary
# studies and from commentary ABOUT guidelines. Two robust signals:
#  (a) a year-anchored "<YYYY> ... guideline(s) for|on ..." pattern — issued
#      clinical guidelines are year-dated society documents whose title reads
#      "2021 ACC/AHA/SCAI Guideline for Coronary Artery Revascularization" /
#      "2024 ESC Guidelines for the management of AF". The phrase "guideline(s)
#      FOR/ON" (not "guideline recommendations for") + a year is the document
#      signal. This catches main-title-only forms (Codex P2: no "for the"
#      required) AND rejects undated guideline-comparison commentary like
#      "International Clinical Practice Guideline Recommendations for Acute PE:
#      Harmony, Dissonance, and Silence" (no year + "guideline recommendations
#      for", not "guideline for") (Codex P1).
#  (b) explicit document-TYPE statements that are themselves the artifact.
# Bare "guideline" / "clinical practice guideline" substrings are DELIBERATELY
# NOT markers (they appear in GDMT primaries + guideline-comparison commentary).
_GUIDELINE_DOC_STATEMENT_MARKERS = (
    "consensus statement", "expert consensus",
    "scientific statement",            # AHA scientific statements
    "position statement",
    "practice bulletin",
)
_GUIDELINE_YEAR_DOC_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(?:19|20)\d{2}\b.{0,80}\bguidelines?\s+(?:for|on|update|focused update)\b"
)
# Primary-study / commentary titles that MENTION guidelines but are NOT
# guideline documents — never promoted (checked FIRST).
_GUIDELINE_TITLE_EXCLUSIONS = (
    "guideline-directed", "guideline directed",      # GDMT — a therapy, studied in primaries
    "guideline adherence", "guideline-adherent", "guideline adherent",
    "adherence to guideline", "adherence to the guideline",
    "guideline-concordant", "guideline concordant",
    "guideline implementation", "guideline-based", "guideline based",
    "guideline-recommended", "guideline recommended",
    "non-guideline", "off-guideline",
)
# iter-5 (Codex P1): study-ABOUT-a-document framings, anchored to the TITLE
# START. A primary study that validates / uses / evaluates a consensus statement,
# decision pathway, or guideline is NOT itself the issued document — e.g.
# "Validation of the 2019 Expert Consensus Algorithm ..." / "Validation of the
# ACC Expert Consensus Decision Pathway ...". Issued guideline/consensus
# documents START with a year or a society name (or "Clinical Practice
# Guideline" / "Expert Consensus ..."), NEVER with a study verb. Anchoring to
# the start (not substring-anywhere) avoids over-excluding real guidelines whose
# scope phrase contains a verb mid-title (e.g. "Guideline for the Evaluation of
# Chest Pain").
_STUDY_FRAMING_TITLE_PREFIXES = (
    "validation of", "validating", "a validation",
    "use of", "using", "utilization of", "utility of",
    "impact of", "implementation of", "application of",
    "evaluation of", "comparison of", "comparing",
    "association of", "association between", "predictors of",
    "outcomes of", "outcomes after", "effect of", "effects of",
    "efficacy and safety of", "efficacy of", "safety of",
    "adherence to", "real-world",
)


def _title_signals_clinical_guideline(title: str) -> bool:
    """I-bug-771 (#812): detect an ISSUED clinical-practice-guideline DOCUMENT
    title for the guideline-authority promotion in Rule 8c. Exclusions checked
    FIRST (GDMT / adherence / implementation primaries). Then a year-anchored
    "guideline(s) for|on" document pattern OR an explicit document-type statement
    (consensus / scientific / position statement / practice bulletin). Rejects
    undated guideline-comparison commentary. Narrower than
    _detect_guideline_or_explainer_title (which fires on explainer/policy titles
    that must stay T4)."""
    if not title:
        return False
    t = title.lower()
    if any(x in t for x in _GUIDELINE_TITLE_EXCLUSIONS):
        return False
    # iter-5: a study ABOUT a document (validation/use/impact/...) starts with a
    # study verb; issued documents do not. Reject these before the document
    # markers so "Validation of the ... Expert Consensus ..." is not promoted.
    t_start = t.lstrip("\"'([ ")
    if any(t_start.startswith(p) for p in _STUDY_FRAMING_TITLE_PREFIXES):
        return False
    if any(m in t for m in _GUIDELINE_DOC_STATEMENT_MARKERS):
        return True
    return bool(_GUIDELINE_YEAR_DOC_RE.search(t))

# M-18a (DR audit pass 1): when the fetcher records the URL as
# doi.org/<prefix>/<suffix>, the classifier cannot know from the
# domain alone that this is a peer-reviewed journal. These DOI
# prefixes are registered to specific peer-reviewed publishers and
# can be trusted as equivalent to PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS
# membership. Source: Crossref registrant records.
PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES = frozenset({
    "10.1056",  # NEJM
    "10.1001",  # JAMA Network
    "10.1016",  # Elsevier (Lancet, Cell, Cell Metabolism, etc.)
    "10.1038",  # Nature Publishing Group
    "10.1126",  # Science / AAAS
    "10.1136",  # BMJ
    "10.1002",  # Wiley
    "10.1007",  # Springer
    "10.1111",  # Wiley
    "10.1210",  # Endocrine Society
    "10.2337",  # American Diabetes Association
    "10.1093",  # Oxford University Press
    "10.1161",  # American Heart Association
    "10.1152",  # American Physiological Society
    "10.4239",  # World Journal of Diabetes
    "10.1172",  # J Clin Invest
    "10.1059",  # Am Coll Physicians / ACP
    "10.3389",  # Frontiers
    "10.3390",  # MDPI
    "10.1371",  # PLOS
    "10.7759",  # Cureus
    "10.1080",  # Taylor & Francis
    "10.1089",  # Mary Ann Liebert
    "10.1155",  # Hindawi (peer-reviewed but mixed quality)
})


def _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(url: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if URL points at a DOI with a peer-reviewed
    publisher prefix (M-18a DR audit fix)."""
    if not url:
        return False
    u = url.lower()
    if "doi.org/" not in u:
        return False
    # Extract DOI after doi.org/
    try:
        tail = u.split("doi.org/", 1)[1]
        prefix = tail.split("/", 1)[0]
        return prefix in PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES
    except (IndexError, ValueError):
        return False


def _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
    """F12 (GH #1245 / D12): True when a doi.org-hosted canonical DOI resolves
    to a real OpenAlex JOURNAL venue.

    THE BUG (run-killer): a canonical-DOI journal hosted on doi.org (e.g. JEP
    10.1257, JPE 10.1086) is NOT on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (host is
    `doi.org`, not the publisher), and its DOI prefix may not be in the
    hard-coded PEER_REVIEWED_DOI_PREFIXES allowlist. R9's unverified-host guard
    then demotes it to T4. ~50 such sources fell to T4 on the workforce corpus,
    collapsing the resolved-venue tier and false-firing abort_corpus_inadequate.

    THE FIX: instead of whack-a-mole prefix expansion, TRUST the resolved
    OpenAlex venue. A doi.org host with OpenAlex `source_type == "journal"` AND
    a non-empty venue name IS a peer-reviewed journal — count it as such rather
    than defaulting to the doi.org-host prior. This widens R9's unverified-host
    EXEMPTION only; it never relaxes a faithfulness gate and is scoped strictly
    to doi.org-hosted canonical DOIs so the BUG-M-11 trade-content guard on
    non-DOI hosts is untouched. SR/MA, narrative, guideline, conference-abstract
    and low-quality-OA branches all run BEFORE / AFTER this exemption and still
    win (e.g. a doi.org/10.3390 MDPI work still hits _is_low_quality_oa -> T4).

    Scope is enforced on the PARSED HOST (host == doi.org or *.doi.org, e.g.
    dx.doi.org), NOT a substring of the URL — a non-doi.org trade host that
    embeds `doi.org/` in its path or query (e.g.
    `https://trade.example/redirect/https://doi.org/10.x`) must NOT pass, so the
    BUG-M-11 trade-content guard stays intact (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1).
    """
    host = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
    if not (host == "doi.org" or host.endswith(".doi.org")):
        return False
    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)


def _is_known_scholarly_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
    """I-arch-011 B17/B11 (KEYSTONE): True when the source is a recognized
    scholarly VENUE on venue/identifier evidence ALONE — independent of how
    much body text the fetcher happened to return.

    This is the shared detector that drives the "do NOT demote a real journal
    to the T7 length floor" carve-out on BOTH classifier paths (the legacy
    rules path R1 and the authority-model path). A source qualifies when ANY
    of these venue signals is present:
      * the host is on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (NEJM / Lancet / BMJ /
        Nature / Brain via academic.oup.com / Movement Disorders via
        onlinelibrary.wiley.com, ...) — parent-domain match;
      * the URL is a doi.org canonical DOI whose registrant prefix is a known
        peer-reviewed publisher (_has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix);
      * the URL is a doi.org canonical DOI that resolved to a real OpenAlex
        JOURNAL venue (_is_doi_org_journal_with_venue);
      * OpenAlex independently resolved the work to a JOURNAL source_type with
        a non-empty venue name.

    It is a VENUE-AUTHORITY signal, NOT a grounding/adequacy signal: a known
    venue keeps its authority weight even when the fetch is an empty stub, but
    the caller MUST set fetch_degraded=True so the stub is excluded from
    grounded-content adequacy downstream (no laundering). Adds no cap/floor —
    it only RESTORES the venue weighting the length-floor was clobbering.
    """
    domain = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
    if _domain_matches(domain, PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS):
        return True
    if _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(signals.url):
        return True
    if _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals):
        return True
    # I-deepfix-001 (U10 tier_venue_exemption, #1344): two gold venue-authority
    # signals ALREADY on the classifier input were ignored, so a real peer-reviewed
    # journal fetched from a BARE-PDF URL (no peer-reviewed venue domain, no doi.org
    # host, no OpenAlex journal source_type stamped on the PDF row) was over-demoted
    # to the T7/UNKNOWN length floor. Both signals are ones a signal-less, merely
    # scholarly-TITLED scam page cannot present: OpenAlex independently resolved the
    # work as peer-reviewed, OR a resolved canonical DOI (registrant prefix "10.").
    # WEIGHT-not-drop (§-1.3): only RESTORES the venue weight the length-floor was
    # clobbering — the caller still sets fetch_degraded=True so the stub is excluded
    # from grounded-content adequacy (no laundering). No cap/floor; engine untouched.
    if signals.openalex_is_peer_reviewed is True:
        return True
    if (signals.doi or "").strip().lower().startswith("10."):
        return True
    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)


def _is_corroborated_scholarly_venue(signals: "ClassificationSignals") -> bool:
    """I-wire-001 (Codex iter-2 P1): STRICTER venue-only predicate for the B2 LLM-tiering
    venue-corroboration cap ONLY. It is deliberately NARROWER than ``_is_known_scholarly_venue``:
    it requires a genuinely RECOGNIZED peer-reviewed VENUE and EXCLUDES both a bare ``doi`` "10."
    prefix and a lone ``openalex_is_peer_reviewed`` flag.

    Why split from ``_is_known_scholarly_venue``: the U10 length-floor widening added a bare-DOI
    ("10.") and a lone peer-reviewed-flag branch so a real journal fetched from a BARE-PDF URL
    keeps its venue WEIGHT at the T7 length floor (a WEIGHT restoration, §-1.3). But that same
    helper is ALSO the B2 corroboration predicate whose entire job is to CAP an uncorroborated
    top-tier (T1/T2) LLM verdict that rests on nothing but a bare DOI + a scholarly-sounding title
    (the off-topic Russian-cosmetics ev_061 mis-tiered T1/0.95 was exactly this). Reusing the
    widened helper there re-opened the cap: a bare-DOI-only row would satisfy corroboration and
    slip its T1/T2 verdict past the cap. So the B2 path uses THIS strict predicate — corroboration
    requires an actual venue signal a signal-less, merely scholarly-TITLED page cannot present:
      * host on PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS (parent-domain match);
      * a doi.org canonical DOI whose registrant prefix is a known peer-reviewed publisher;
      * a doi.org canonical DOI resolved to a real OpenAlex JOURNAL venue;
      * OpenAlex resolved the work to a JOURNAL source_type WITH a non-empty venue name.
    A bare ``doi`` "10." and a lone ``openalex_is_peer_reviewed`` are NOT venues, so they do NOT
    corroborate. WEIGHT-only (§-1.3): the cap this predicate guards can ONLY lower an
    uncorroborated top tier to the deterministic rules-floor — it never raises a tier, never drops
    a source, never gates release. The faithfulness engine is untouched."""
    domain = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
    if _domain_matches(domain, PEER_REVIEWED_JOURNAL_DOMAINS):
        return True
    if _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix(signals.url):
        return True
    if _is_doi_org_journal_with_venue(signals):
        return True
    src_type = (signals.openalex_source_type or "").strip().lower()
    venue = (signals.openalex_venue or "").strip()
    return src_type == "journal" and bool(venue)


def _is_low_quality_oa(domain: str, url: str) -> bool:
    """I-bug-771 (#812): True if the source is a low-quality / high-volume OA
    publisher (MDPI) by domain OR by URL-embedded DOI prefix. Used to deny T1
    primary credit in R9/R10 (SR/MA still routes to T2 in the earlier branch)."""
    if _domain_matches(domain, LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOMAINS):
        return True
    u = (url or "").lower()
    # Exact DOI-prefix match (iter-2 Codex P2: avoid substring false-positives
    # such as 10.33901 matching 10.3390). Extract the registrant prefix after
    # doi.org/ exactly, mirroring _has_peer_reviewed_doi_prefix.
    if "doi.org/" in u:
        try:
            prefix = u.split("doi.org/", 1)[1].split("/", 1)[0]
            if prefix in LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOI_PREFIXES:
                return True
        except (IndexError, ValueError):
            pass
    # DOI embedded in a publisher path (e.g. /10.3390/...): require both
    # boundary slashes so 10.33901 cannot match 10.3390.
    return any(f"/{p}/" in u for p in LOW_QUALITY_OA_DOI_PREFIXES)


# Preprint servers (T4/T5 candidates — not peer-reviewed; caller may
# tier-down further based on funding disclosure).
PREPRINT_DOMAINS = frozenset({
    "arxiv.org", "biorxiv.org", "medrxiv.org", "ssrn.com",
    "preprints.org", "researchsquare.com", "papers.ssrn.com",
    "osf.io",
})

# Student-journal / low-credibility-journal patterns. Plan A-S3-A note
# flagged nhsjs.com (National High School Journal of Science) as
# inappropriately tagged GOLD in PG_LB_SA_02. These are not predatory
# per se but don't meet T1/T2 peer-review standards.
STUDENT_JOURNAL_DOMAINS = frozenset({
    "nhsjs.com",
})

# Abstract-only / stub-content domains where typical content is just a
# title + abstract. Used as a hint; falls through to content-length check.
ABSTRACT_ONLY_DOMAINS = frozenset({
    "semanticscholar.org",
    "openalex.org",
})

# Content-length thresholds (chars of fetched body).
MIN_T1_T4_CONTENT_CHARS = 3000  # below this, downgrade to T7 stub
T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS = 1000    # below this, always T7 stub


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Helpers
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def _normalize_domain(url: str) -> str:
    """Extract lowercase domain from URL, stripping www. and path."""
    if not url:
        return ""
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}")
        host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower()
        return host[4:] if host.startswith("www.") else host
    except Exception:
        return ""


def _domain_matches(domain: str, domain_set: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
    """Check whether domain (or any parent) is in the set."""
    if not domain:
        return False
    if domain in domain_set:
        return True
    # Parent-domain match: foo.fda.gov matches fda.gov
    parts = domain.split(".")
    for i in range(len(parts)):
        parent = ".".join(parts[i:])
        if parent in domain_set:
            return True
    return False


def _detect_systematic_review_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
    """Heuristic: title contains 'systematic review' / 'meta-analysis'."""
    if not title:
        return False
    t = title.lower()
    return any(k in t for k in (
        "systematic review", "meta-analysis", "meta analysis",
        "network meta-analysis", "cochrane review", "umbrella review",
        "scoping review",
    ))


def _detect_narrative_review_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
    """Heuristic: title contains 'review' but not systematic / meta."""
    if not title:
        return False
    t = title.lower()
    if "review" in t and not _detect_systematic_review_from_title(title):
        return True
    # Commentary / editorial / perspective markers
    return any(k in t for k in (
        "commentary", "editorial", "perspective", "opinion",
        "viewpoint",
    ))


def _detect_conference_abstract(title: str, url: str = "") -> bool:
    """Heuristic: title / URL signals a conference abstract.

    Matches:
    - explicit keywords (conference abstract, poster, oral presentation)
    - "1745-P:" / "82-OR:" / "P-123:" abstract-number prefixes commonly
      used by ADA, ENDO, ESC, etc.
    - Pass-16 (Codex full-scale pass 1): Endocrine Society / ENDO
      day-prefixed presentation IDs like "THU296" / "MON-123" / "FRI42"
      / "SAT-56" / "SUN-78" that prefix supplement abstracts.
    - URLs containing "/Supplement_" or "/abstract/" or journal-issue
      supplement paths, or "/jes/article-pdf/.../Supplement_"
    """
    if title:
        t = title.lower()
        if any(k in t for k in (
            "conference abstract", "poster", "oral presentation",
            "abstract p", "abstract po",
        )):
            return True
        # Numbered abstract prefix (e.g., "1745-P: Long-Term Safety...")
        if re.match(r"^\s*\d+-[A-Z]+:", title):
            return True
        # Numbered "P-" prefix
        if re.match(r"^\s*P-\d+", title):
            return True
        # Pass-16 (Codex full-scale pass 1): Endocrine Society day-letter
        # presentation IDs. Pattern: MON/TUE/WED/THU/FRI/SAT/SUN + 2-4
        # digits, optionally with hyphen. Must be at title start.
        if re.match(
            r"^\s*(MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI|SAT|SUN)-?\d{2,4}\b",
            title, re.IGNORECASE,
        ):
            return True
        # Pass-16: abstract-ID-like prefixes commonly seen on JES/JCEM
        # supplements, e.g., "OR01-2", "OR30-04", "SUN-245"
        if re.match(r"^\s*OR\d+-\d+\b", title, re.IGNORECASE):
            return True
    if url:
        u = url.lower()
        if "/supplement_" in u or "/supplement/" in u:
            return True
        # Pass-16: "/jes/article-pdf/.../Supplement_1/..." pattern
        if "/article-pdf/" in u and "supplement" in u:
            return True
    return False


# Narrative-review / commentary-flavored keywords. Broader than the
# "systematic review" family. Surfaces commentary-ish articles in
# peer-reviewed venues (J Obes Metab Syndr, Postgraduate Med, etc.).
# M-18a (DR audit pass 1): STRONG narrative markers — always fire
# regardless of primary-study signals. These phrases unambiguously
# indicate that the paper is NOT a primary RCT even if it references
# one (e.g., post-hoc analyses of a SURPASS trial ARE narrative).
_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_STRONG_MARKERS = (
    # Case reports / series — always narrative regardless of primary terms
    "case report", "case reports", "case study",
    "a case of", "a case report",
    # Secondary / post-hoc analyses — always narrative; the primary
    # paper is the parent RCT, not the post-hoc report
    "post hoc", "post-hoc", "post hoc analysis", "post-hoc analysis",
    "secondary analysis",
    "pooled analysis",
    "subgroup analysis",
    # Program-level pooled reporting — still narrative
    "in the step program", "in the step programme",
    "in the surpass program", "in the surmount program",
    "in the rewind program", "in the leader program",
    "in the sustain program", "in the pioneer program",
    "in the select program",
    # M-18c (Codex pass 10 advisory): narrative framings about
    # randomized trials are reviews ABOUT RCTs, not RCTs themselves.
    # These must fire even when the title also contains "randomized".
    "beyond randomized", "beyond rcts",
    "update on randomized", "update on rcts",
    "the role of randomized", "role of randomized",
    "interpreting randomized", "interpreting rcts",
    "overview of randomized", "overview of rcts",
)

# M-18a: WEAK narrative markers — defer to primary-study signals when
# present. These phrases can appear in both narrative reviews AND in
# primary RCT titles (e.g., NEJM "X as Compared with Y for the
# Treatment of Z" uses "for the treatment of" as a study population
# delimiter, not as a narrative frame).
_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_WEAK_MARKERS = (
    "a game changer", "game-changer", "game changer",
    "update on ", "the role of ",
    "overview of ", "advances in ",
    "a review of ",
    "the upcoming", "the coming", "on the horizon",
    "perspectives on", "viewpoints on",
    "against obesity", "for obesity",
    "for the treatment of", "for the management of",
    "perspective for", "perspective on ", "a perspective",
    "perspectives for",
    "primary care providers", "primary care physician",
    "for clinicians", "for physicians",
    "prescribing ",
    "what the clinician", "what clinicians",
)

# Preserve the combined tuple for backwards compatibility with any
# downstream code that imports _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_KEYWORDS directly.
_NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_KEYWORDS = (
    _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_STRONG_MARKERS + _NARRATIVE_FLAVOR_WEAK_MARKERS
)


def _detect_narrative_flavor_from_title(title: str) -> bool:
    """Stronger-than-generic detector for narrative / commentary flavor.

    M-18a (DR audit pass 1): split narrative markers into STRONG (case
    report, post-hoc analysis, pooled analysis, program-level) and
    WEAK (for the treatment of, update on, perspective for). Strong
    markers always fire. Weak markers defer to primary-study signals
    so NEJM head-to-head RCTs like "Tirzepatide as Compared with
    Semaglutide for the Treatment of Obesity" are not demoted.
    """

109	def _compute_perspective_distribution(
110	    evidence: list[dict],
111	) -> tuple[dict, list[str]]:
112	    """RC-7: Compute Shannon entropy and identify underrepresented perspectives.
113	
114	    Returns:
115	        (distribution_info, underrepresented_perspectives) where:
116	        - distribution_info: {"entropy": float, "counts": {perspective: count}}
117	        - underrepresented_perspectives: perspectives with < min_pct coverage
118	    """
119	    import math
120	    from collections import Counter
121	
122	    min_pct = float(resolve("PG_V3_MIN_PERSPECTIVE_PCT"))
123	
124	    perspectives = [e.get("perspective", "Unknown") for e in evidence]
125	    counts = Counter(perspectives)
126	    total = sum(counts.values())
127	
128	    if total == 0:
129	        return {"entropy": 0.0, "counts": {}}, list(STORM_PERSPECTIVES)
130	
131	    # Shannon entropy
132	    entropy = -sum(
133	        (c / total) * math.log2(c / total)
134	        for c in counts.values() if c > 0
135	    )
136	    max_entropy = math.log2(len(STORM_PERSPECTIVES)) if STORM_PERSPECTIVES else 1.0
137	    normalized_entropy = entropy / max_entropy if max_entropy > 0 else 0.0
138	
139	    # Underrepresented: below min_pct of total
140	    underrepresented = [
141	        p for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES
142	        if counts.get(p, 0) / max(total, 1) < min_pct
143	    ]
144	
145	    return {
146	        "entropy": round(normalized_entropy, 3),
147	        "counts": dict(counts),
148	    }, underrepresented

167	def _search_openalex(query: str, max_results: int = 10) -> list[dict]:
168	    """FIX-047-K11: Search OpenAlex for academic papers.
169	
170	    OpenAlex is a free, open alternative to Semantic Scholar with 474M+ works.
171	    Uses the REST API directly (no pyalex dependency required).
172	
173	    Returns list of dicts matching the polaris graph search result format:
174	    {url, title, snippet, source_type, year, authors, venue, doi, ...}
175	    """
176	    import urllib.parse
177	    import urllib.request
178	    import json as _json
179	
180	    encoded_query = urllib.parse.quote(query)
181	    # Use polite pool (faster) by including email
182	    api_url = (
183	        f"https://api.openalex.org/works?"
184	        f"search={encoded_query}"
185	        f"&per_page={min(max_results, 25)}"
186	        f"&sort=relevance_score:desc"
187	        f"&select=id,doi,title,publication_year,authorships,"
188	        f"primary_location,abstract_inverted_index,cited_by_count,type,"
189	        f"is_retracted,open_access"
190	        f"&mailto={PG_OPENALEX_EMAIL}"
191	    )
192	
193	    try:
194	        req = urllib.request.Request(api_url, headers={"User-Agent": "POLARIS/1.0"})
195	        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
196	            data = _json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
197	    except Exception as exc:
198	        logger.warning(
199	            "[polaris graph] FIX-047-K11: OpenAlex API failed: %s",
200	            str(exc)[:200],
201	        )
202	        return []
203	
204	    results = []
205	    for work in data.get("results", []):
206	        # Reconstruct abstract from inverted index
207	        abstract = ""
208	        inv_idx = work.get("abstract_inverted_index")
209	        if inv_idx and isinstance(inv_idx, dict):
210	            # inverted_index: {"word": [pos1, pos2, ...]}
211	            word_positions: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
212	            for word, positions in inv_idx.items():
213	                for pos in positions:
214	                    word_positions.append((pos, word))
215	            word_positions.sort()
216	            abstract = " ".join(w for _, w in word_positions)
217	
218	        # Extract authors
219	        authors = []
220	        for authorship in (work.get("authorships") or [])[:5]:
221	            author = authorship.get("author", {})
222	            name = author.get("display_name", "")
223	            if name:
224	                authors.append(name)
225	
226	        # Extract venue (FIX-B14: use primary_location, host_venue deprecated)
227	        venue = ""
228	        _prim_loc = work.get("primary_location") or {}
229	        _source = _prim_loc.get("source") or {}
230	        venue = _source.get("display_name", "")
231	
232	        # Extract URL (prefer DOI, then landing page)
233	        doi = work.get("doi", "")
234	        url = doi if doi else work.get("id", "")
235	        primary_loc = work.get("primary_location") or {}
236	        if primary_loc.get("pdf_url"):
237	            url = primary_loc["pdf_url"]
238	        elif primary_loc.get("landing_page_url"):
239	            url = primary_loc["landing_page_url"]
240	
241	        title = work.get("title", "")
242	        if not title:
243	            continue
244	
245	        results.append({
246	            "url": url,
247	            "title": title,
248	            "snippet": abstract[:500] if abstract else title,
249	            "source_type": "academic",
250	            "year": work.get("publication_year"),
251	            "authors": authors,
252	            "venue": venue,
253	            "doi": doi.replace("https://doi.org/", "") if doi else "",
254	            "citation_count": work.get("cited_by_count", 0),
255	            "openalex_id": work.get("id", ""),
256	        })
257	
258	    return results[:max_results]
259

275	async def execute_searches(
276	    state: ResearchState,
277	    client=None,
278	) -> dict:
279	    """
280	    Execute all web and academic searches in parallel.
281	
282	    Returns state update with web_results and academic_results.
283	    """
284	    sub_queries = state["sub_queries"]
285	    region = state["region"]
286	
287	    if not sub_queries:
288	        logger.warning("[polaris graph] No sub-queries to search")
289	        return {"web_results": [], "academic_results": []}
290	
291	    # Agentic search loop: when enabled, replaces the static batch pipeline
292	    if PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED and client:
293	        return await execute_agentic_search(state, client)
294	
295	    web_search_fn, academic_search_fn = _import_search_tools()
296	
297	    # Change 2: Query amplification — expand queries for broader web coverage.
298	    # I-ready-017 FX-19 (#1127): this is the LEGACY STATIC PATH. It is unreachable under the
299	    # agentic slate (the early return above at PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED branch hands off to
300	    # execute_agentic_search before this point), so PG_AMPLIFICATION_VARIANTS is INERT on the
301	    # benchmark. Retained only for the non-agentic lane (PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED=0).
302	    if PG_AMPLIFICATION_ENABLED:
303	        try:
304	            amplify_fn = _import_amplifier()
305	            original_count = len(sub_queries)
306	            amplified = amplify_fn(sub_queries, region=region)
307	            # Cap amplified queries to prevent explosion
308	            max_amplified = original_count * PG_AMPLIFICATION_VARIANTS
309	            web_queries = amplified[:max_amplified]
310	            logger.info(
311	                "[polaris graph] Query amplification: %d -> %d queries "
312	                "(cap=%d, variants=%d)",
313	                original_count,
314	                len(web_queries),
315	                max_amplified,
316	                PG_AMPLIFICATION_VARIANTS,
317	            )
318	            # OBS-3: Trace query amplification (WAVE-2.4: include full query strings)
319	            tracer = get_tracer()
320	            if tracer:
321	                tracer.query("search", "amplified", web_queries,
322	                    queries=[q for q in web_queries])
323	        except Exception as exc:
324	            logger.warning(
325	                "[polaris graph] Amplification failed: %s — using original queries",
326	                str(exc)[:200],
327	            )
328	            web_queries = sub_queries
329	    else:
330	        web_queries = sub_queries
331	
332	    # Academic: use original queries (precise terminology), capped
333	    academic_queries = sub_queries[:PG_ACADEMIC_QUERY_CAP]
334	
335	    logger.info(
336	        "[polaris graph] Searching: %d web queries (concurrency=%d), "
337	        "%d academic queries (concurrency=%d)",
338	        len(web_queries),
339	        WEB_CONCURRENCY,
340	        len(academic_queries),
341	        ACADEMIC_CONCURRENCY,
342	    )
343	
344	    # FIX-PARALLEL: Run web, academic, and Exa searches in parallel.
345	    # They hit different APIs (Serper, S2, Exa) so no rate-limit conflict.
346	    # DDG fallback and citation chase remain sequential (conditional on results).
347	    logger.info(
348	        "[polaris graph] FIX-PARALLEL: Launching web + academic + exa "
349	        "searches in parallel"
350	    )
351	
352	    web_task = asyncio.create_task(
353	        _adaptive_web_search(
354	            web_search_fn, web_queries, region,
355	            client=client,
356	            original_query=state["original_query"],
357	        )
358	    )
359	    academic_task = asyncio.create_task(
360	        _run_academic_searches(academic_search_fn, academic_queries)
361	    )
362	    exa_task = asyncio.create_task(
363	        _run_exa_searches(sub_queries)
364	    )
365	
366	    # Gather all three — return_exceptions so one failure doesn't kill others
367	    web_result, academic_result, exa_result = await asyncio.gather(
368	        web_task, academic_task, exa_task, return_exceptions=True,
369	    )
370	
371	    # I-ready-019 FL-04 (#1104): gather(return_exceptions=True) captures a STRUCTURAL discovery 404
372	    # as a result ELEMENT — re-raise it BEFORE the per-lane Exception->[] conversion below, so a dead
373	    # discovery LLM fails the run loud instead of silently degrading to empty results (LAW II).
374	    for _gathered in (web_result, academic_result, exa_result):
375	        if isinstance(_gathered, NoEndpointError):
376	            raise _gathered
377	
378	    # Handle web results
379	    if isinstance(web_result, Exception):
380	        logger.error(
381	            "[polaris graph] FIX-PARALLEL: Web search failed: %s",
382	            str(web_result)[:200],
383	        )
384	        web_results = []
385	    else:
386	        web_results = web_result
387	
388	    # Handle academic results
389	    if isinstance(academic_result, Exception):
390	        logger.error(
391	            "[polaris graph] FIX-PARALLEL: Academic search failed: %s",
392	            str(academic_result)[:200],
393	        )
394	        academic_results = []
395	    else:
396	        academic_results = academic_result
397	
398	    # Handle Exa results
399	    if isinstance(exa_result, Exception):
400	        logger.warning(
401	            "[polaris graph] FIX-PARALLEL: Exa search failed: %s",
402	            str(exa_result)[:200],
403	        )
404	    elif exa_result:
405	        web_results.extend(exa_result)
406	
407	    # Change 6: DuckDuckGo fallback for zero-result queries (sequential, conditional)
408	    web_results = await _run_ddg_fallback_for_zeros(
409	        web_queries, web_results, region
410	    )
411	
412	    # FIX-306: Citation chasing — follow references from top academic papers
413	    # IMP-3: Pass query for embedding-based relevance filtering
414	    # Must run after academic search completes (needs paper IDs)
415	    if PG_CITATION_CHASE_ENABLED and academic_results:
416	        chased = await _chase_citations(
417	            academic_results, query=state["original_query"]
418	        )
419	        if chased:
420	            academic_results.extend(chased)
421	
422	    # Deduplicate by URL
423	    web_results = _deduplicate_results(web_results, key="url")
424	    academic_results = _deduplicate_results(academic_results, key="url")
425	
426	    logger.info(
427	        "[polaris graph] Search complete: %d web results, %d academic results",
428	        len(web_results),
429	        len(academic_results),
430	    )
431	
432	    return {
433	        "web_results": web_results,
434	        "academic_results": academic_results,
435	        "status": "analyzing",
436	    }

439	async def _run_web_searches(
440	    search_fn,
441	    queries: list[str],
442	    region: str,
443	) -> list[dict]:
444	    """Run web searches with concurrency control."""
445	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(WEB_CONCURRENCY)
446	    results: list[dict] = []
447	    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
448	
449	    async def _search(query: str):
450	        async with semaphore:
451	            # AREA-5: Check search cache before API call
452	            cached = await get_cached_results(query, "serper")
453	            if cached is not None:
454	                for r in cached:
455	                    r["search_query"] = query
456	                    r["source_type"] = "web"
457	                logger.debug(
458	                    "[polaris graph] AREA-5: Search cache hit for '%s' (%d results)",
459	                    query[:50], len(cached),
460	                )
461	                # WAVE-2.2: Trace cache hit
462	                tracer = get_tracer()
463	                if tracer:
464	                    tracer.search_result("search", "serper", query, len(cached), cached=True)
465	                return cached
466	
467	            try:
468	                # web_search is sync, run in thread pool
469	                batch = await loop.run_in_executor(
470	                    _executor,
471	                    lambda: search_fn.invoke({
472	                        "query": query,
473	                        "max_results": WEB_RESULTS_PER_QUERY,
474	                        "region": region,
475	                    }),
476	                )
477	                if isinstance(batch, list):
478	                    for r in batch:
479	                        r["search_query"] = query
480	                        r["source_type"] = "web"
481	                    # AREA-5: Cache successful search results
482	                    await cache_results(query, batch, "serper")
483	                    # OBS-3: Trace per-query web results (WAVE-2.2: full URLs/titles/snippets)
484	                    tracer = get_tracer()
485	                    if tracer:
486	                        tracer.search_result("search", "serper", query, len(batch),
487	                            urls=[r.get("url", "") for r in batch[:10]],
488	                            titles=[r.get("title", "") for r in batch[:10]],
489	                            snippets=[r.get("snippet", "") for r in batch[:10]])
490	                    return batch
491	            except Exception as exc:
492	                logger.warning(
493	                    "[polaris graph] Web search failed for '%s': %s",
494	                    query[:50],
495	                    str(exc)[:100],
496	                )
497	            return []
498	
499	    tasks = [_search(q) for q in queries]
500	    batch_results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
501	
502	    for batch in batch_results:
503	        results.extend(batch)
504	
505	    return results

509	# FIX-CITE-3/S1: Synonym expansion for academic topic overlap.
510	# The stem-only approach rejected 926/926 papers because "time-restricted
511	# eating", "caloric restriction", "metabolic syndrome" have zero stem overlap
512	# with "intermittent fasting". These synonym sets ensure semantically
513	# equivalent research terms are recognized as matching.
514	_SYNONYM_SETS: list[frozenset[str]] = [
515	    frozenset(["fast", "eat", "diet", "feed", "nourish", "calor", "restrict"]),
516	    frozenset(["intermitt", "time-restrict", "period", "cycl", "alternat"]),
517	    frozenset(["metabol", "glycem", "insulin", "glucos", "lipid", "cholesterol"]),
518	    frozenset(["cardiovascular", "cardiac", "heart", "coronar", "vascular"]),
519	    frozenset(["obes", "overweight", "adipos", "weight", "body mass", "bmi"]),
520	    frozenset(["mortal", "death", "surviv", "longev"]),
521	    frozenset(["random", "clinical trial", "rct", "controlled trial"]),
522	    frozenset(["meta-analys", "systematic review", "umbrella review"]),
523	    frozenset(["inflammat", "cytokine", "crp", "il-6", "tnf"]),
524	    frozenset(["blood pressur", "hypertens", "systolic", "diastolic"]),
525	]
526	
527	
528	def _expand_with_synonyms(stems: set[str]) -> set[str]:
529	    """Expand a set of stems with synonyms from _SYNONYM_SETS.
530	
531	    Uses minimum 5-char prefix matching to avoid false positives
532	    (e.g., "system" matching "systematic review" via 4-char prefix).
533	    """
534	    expanded = set(stems)
535	    _min_prefix = 5
536	    for syn_set in _SYNONYM_SETS:
537	        matched = False
538	        for stem in stems:
539	            if matched:
540	                break
541	            for syn in syn_set:
542	                # Skip multi-word synonyms for substring matching
543	                # (prevents "system" matching "systematic review")
544	                is_multi_word = " " in syn
545	                # Require longer prefix match to avoid false positives
546	                prefix_len = max(_min_prefix, min(len(stem), len(syn)))
547	                if not is_multi_word and stem[:prefix_len] == syn[:prefix_len]:
548	                    expanded.update(syn_set)
549	                    matched = True
550	                    break
551	                # Single-word: check containment (min 5 chars to avoid noise)
552	                if not is_multi_word and len(syn) >= 5 and (syn in stem or stem in syn):
553	                    expanded.update(syn_set)
554	                    matched = True
555	                    break
556	                # Multi-word: require exact match in the stems set
557	                if is_multi_word and syn in " ".join(sorted(stems)):
558	                    expanded.update(syn_set)
559	                    matched = True
560	                    break
561	    return expanded
562	
563	
564	def _prefilter_academic_results(papers: list[dict], query: str) -> list[dict]:
565	    """FIX-059-E: Pre-filter academic results before evidence extraction.
566	
567	    Rejects papers where:
568	    1. No abstract or abstract too short (<50 chars) (H-12)
569	    2. Zero stemmed-word overlap between query and title+abstract (BUG-5)
570	
571	    FIX-CITE-3/S1: Uses synonym expansion so "time-restricted eating",
572	    "caloric restriction", "metabolic syndrome" etc. match queries about
573	    "intermittent fasting". Previously rejected 926/926 relevant papers.
574	    """
575	
576	    def _stem_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
577	        """Simple stemming: lowercase, strip common suffixes."""
578	        words = set(re.findall(r"\b[a-z]{3,}\b", text.lower()))
579	        stemmed: set[str] = set()
580	        for w in words:
581	            for suffix in (
582	                "tion", "sion", "ment", "ness", "ing",
583	                "ed", "ly", "er", "est", "ies", "es", "s",
584	            ):
585	                if w.endswith(suffix) and len(w) - len(suffix) >= 3:
586	                    w = w[: -len(suffix)]
587	                    break
588	            stemmed.add(w)
589	        return stemmed
590	
591	    min_abstract_len = int(resolve("PG_ACADEMIC_MIN_ABSTRACT_LEN"))
592	
593	    query_stems = _stem_words(query)
594	    # FIX-CITE-3/S1: Expand query stems with synonyms
595	    expanded_query_stems = _expand_with_synonyms(query_stems)
596	
597	    filtered: list[dict] = []
598	    rejected_no_abstract = 0
599	    rejected_no_overlap = 0
600	
601	    for paper in papers:
602	        # H-12: Skip papers without meaningful abstracts
603	        # FIX-CITE-3/S4: OpenAlex uses "snippet" not "abstract". S2 uses "abstract".
604	        abstract = paper.get("abstract", "") or paper.get("snippet", "") or ""
605	        if len(abstract.strip()) < min_abstract_len:
606	            rejected_no_abstract += 1
607	            continue
608	
609	        # BUG-5: Check title/abstract has topic overlap with expanded query stems.
610	        # FIX-CITE-3/S1: Use synonym expansion for bidirectional matching.
611	        # FIX-074: Lowered from 2 to 1 synonym group. The 2-group requirement
612	        # blocked 80-100% of academic results across TEST_068-074 because
613	        # OpenAlex papers about "geographic variations of fasting" only match
614	        # the fasting group, not a second medical group. The original false
615	        # positive (UAV radar) has ZERO matching groups, so 1-group suffices.
616	        _min_overlap = 1
617	        title = paper.get("title", "") or ""
618	        title_stems = _stem_words(title)
619	        expanded_title = _expand_with_synonyms(title_stems)
620	        overlap = expanded_query_stems & expanded_title
621	        # Also count how many distinct synonym sets are represented
622	        _overlap_groups = sum(
623	            1 for syn_set in _SYNONYM_SETS
624	            if overlap & syn_set
625	        )
626	        if _overlap_groups < _min_overlap:
627	            # Fallback: check abstract for overlap
628	            abstract_stems = _stem_words(abstract[:300])
629	            expanded_abstract = _expand_with_synonyms(abstract_stems)
630	            abstract_overlap = expanded_query_stems & expanded_abstract
631	            _abs_groups = sum(
632	                1 for syn_set in _SYNONYM_SETS
633	                if abstract_overlap & syn_set
634	            )
635	            if _abs_groups < _min_overlap:
636	                rejected_no_overlap += 1
637	                continue
638	
639	        filtered.append(paper)
640	
641	    total_rejected = rejected_no_abstract + rejected_no_overlap
642	    if total_rejected > 0:
643	        logger.info(
644	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-E: Pre-filtered %d/%d academic results "
645	            "(no_abstract=%d, no_overlap=%d)",
646	            total_rejected,
647	            len(papers),
648	            rejected_no_abstract,
649	            rejected_no_overlap,
650	        )
651	
652	    return filtered
653
