content.text: Excellent! Let me read the constraint_extractor:

content.content:
1	"""Instruction Constraint Extractor.
2	
3	A general, prompt-agnostic LLM pass that reads a free-text task prompt and
4	returns the concrete deliverable constraints the author must honor. It is
5	adversarially prompted to surface BURIED constraints — rules phrased
6	mid-sentence, tucked into a subordinate clause, or dropped as a soft aside
7	("ideally...", "where possible...", "and please keep it...") — not just the
8	obvious headline ask.
9	
10	Design notes
11	------------
12	* Standalone module. Nothing here is wired into the driver or generator; the
13	  caller decides what (if anything) to do with the returned dict.
14	* Reuses the repo's existing LLM gateway (``OpenRouterClient.generate``). No
15	  API keys are hardcoded — the client reads ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` / model
16	  from the environment exactly as every other module does.
17	* The live LLM call is split from the pure parser. ``parse_constraints_json``
18	  turns a raw model string into a normalized :class:`Constraints`, so the shape
19	  can be unit-tested with a fixture and no network.
20	
21	Typed fields (all keys always present in the returned dict)
22	-----------------------------------------------------------
23	``source_types``     list[str]  e.g. ['journal_article']
24	``languages``        list[str]  e.g. ['en'] (ISO-639-1 where known)
25	``recency``          str|None   a cutoff phrase/date, e.g. 'since 2020', or None
26	``required_coverage``list[str]  topical slots the prompt implies/names
27	``exclusions``       list[str]  things to exclude (source kinds, topics, ...)
28	``format``           str|None   e.g. 'literature_review'
29	``length``           str|None   e.g. '3000 words' / '10 pages', or None
30	``tone``             str|None   e.g. 'academic', or None
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import json
36	import logging
37	import os
38	import re
39	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
40	from typing import Any, Optional
41	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Canonical field set + normalization vocab
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	# The full, ordered set of keys the extractor guarantees on every result dict.
50	_FIELDS: tuple[str, ...] = (
51	    "source_types",
52	    "languages",
53	    "recency",
54	    "required_coverage",
55	    "exclusions",
56	    "format",
57	    "length",
58	    "tone",
59	)
60	
61	_LIST_FIELDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
62	    {"source_types", "languages", "required_coverage", "exclusions"}
63	)
64	_SCALAR_FIELDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"recency", "format", "length", "tone"})
65	
66	# Light, deterministic canonicalization so common phrasings collapse to stable
67	# tokens the rest of the pipeline can match on. This is intentionally shallow —
68	# the LLM does the semantic lifting; this only tidies synonyms it emits.
69	_SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
70	    "journal article": "journal_article",
71	    "journal articles": "journal_article",
72	    "journal-article": "journal_article",
73	    "journal": "journal_article",
74	    "peer reviewed journal article": "journal_article",
75	    "peer-reviewed journal article": "journal_article",
76	    "peer reviewed article": "peer_reviewed",
77	    "peer-reviewed": "peer_reviewed",
78	    "peer reviewed": "peer_reviewed",
79	    "academic paper": "journal_article",
80	    "scholarly article": "journal_article",
81	    "conference paper": "conference_paper",
82	    "working paper": "working_paper",
83	    "preprint": "preprint",
84	    "book": "book",
85	    "book chapter": "book_chapter",
86	    "report": "report",
87	    "government report": "government_report",
88	    "news article": "news_article",
89	    "news": "news_article",
90	    "blog": "blog_post",
91	    "blog post": "blog_post",
92	    "website": "website",
93	    "grey literature": "grey_literature",
94	    "gray literature": "grey_literature",
95	}
96	
97	# ISO-639-1 canonicalization for the languages the prompt is likely to name.
98	_LANGUAGE_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
99	    "english": "en",
100	    "en": "en",
101	    "eng": "en",
102	    "english-language": "en",
103	    "english language": "en",
104	    "spanish": "es",
105	    "french": "fr",
106	    "german": "de",
107	    "chinese": "zh",
108	    "mandarin": "zh",
109	    "japanese": "ja",
110	    "portuguese": "pt",
111	    "italian": "it",
112	    "russian": "ru",
113	    "arabic": "ar",
114	    "korean": "ko",
115	    "dutch": "nl",
116	}
117	
118	_FORMAT_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
119	    "literature review": "literature_review",
120	    "lit review": "literature_review",
121	    "systematic review": "systematic_review",
122	    "scoping review": "scoping_review",
123	    "narrative review": "narrative_review",
124	    "meta analysis": "meta_analysis",
125	    "meta-analysis": "meta_analysis",
126	    "essay": "essay",
127	    "report": "report",
128	    "white paper": "white_paper",
129	    "policy brief": "policy_brief",
130	    "briefing": "briefing",
131	    "summary": "summary",
132	    "blog post": "blog_post",
133	    "article": "article",
134	}
135	
136	
137	@dataclass
138	class Constraints:
139	    """Normalized constraint bundle.
140	
141	    Every field is populated (lists default empty, scalars default ``None``) so
142	    downstream code never has to guard for a missing key.
143	    """
144	
145	    source_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
146	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
147	    recency: Optional[str] = None
148	    required_coverage: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
149	    exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
150	    format: Optional[str] = None
151	    length: Optional[str] = None
152	    tone: Optional[str] = None
153	
154	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
155	        """Return the canonical dict with all keys present, in field order."""
156	        return {
157	            "source_types": list(self.source_types),
158	            "languages": list(self.languages),
159	            "recency": self.recency,
160	            "required_coverage": list(self.required_coverage),
161	            "exclusions": list(self.exclusions),
162	            "format": self.format,
163	            "length": self.length,
164	            "tone": self.tone,
165	        }
166	
167	
168	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
169	# Prompt (adversarial — hunt buried constraints)
170	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
173	    "You are a meticulous requirements analyst. Your job is to read a task "
174	    "prompt written for a report author and extract EVERY constraint the "
175	    "author must obey to produce an acceptable deliverable.\n\n"
176	    "Be adversarial about BURIED constraints. Constraints are frequently NOT "
177	    "stated as a clean bullet list — they hide:\n"
178	    "  * mid-sentence ('...citing only high-quality, English-language journal "
179	    "articles.')\n"
180	    "  * in subordinate clauses ('which should exclude opinion pieces')\n"
181	    "  * as soft asides ('ideally peer-reviewed', 'where possible since 2020', "
182	    "'and please keep the tone academic')\n"
183	    "  * implied by the deliverable type (a 'literature review' implies scholarly "
184	    "sources and a review structure).\n\n"
185	    "Extract only what the prompt actually asks for or clearly implies. Do NOT "
186	    "invent constraints that are not supported by the text. If a field is not "
187	    "constrained, leave it empty/null — do not guess.\n\n"
188	    "Return STRICT JSON only (no prose, no code fences) with EXACTLY these keys:\n"
189	    "  source_types: array of strings — kinds of sources allowed/required "
190	    "(e.g. 'journal_article', 'peer_reviewed'). If the prompt restricts to a "
191	    "source kind ('only ... journal articles'), list ONLY that kind.\n"
192	    "  languages: array of strings — required source languages (prefer ISO-639-1 "
193	    "like 'en').\n"
194	    "  recency: string or null — any recency/date-window rule (e.g. 'since 2020', "
195	    "'last 5 years').\n"
196	    "  required_coverage: array of strings — specific topics/sub-questions/slots "
197	    "the review must cover, as named or clearly implied by the prompt.\n"
198	    "  exclusions: array of strings — anything to exclude (source kinds, topics, "
199	    "regions).\n"
200	    "  format: string or null — the deliverable format (e.g. 'literature_review', "
201	    "'systematic_review', 'report', 'essay').\n"
202	    "  length: string or null — any length/word/page target.\n"
203	    "  tone: string or null — required tone/register (e.g. 'academic', 'formal').\n"
204	)
205	
206	_USER_TEMPLATE = (
207	    "Extract the deliverable constraints from the following task prompt.\n"
208	    "Read it twice: once for the obvious headline ask, and once hunting for "
209	    "constraints buried mid-sentence or phrased as soft asides.\n\n"
210	    "TASK PROMPT:\n"
211	    '"""\n{prompt}\n"""\n\n'
212	    "Return the strict JSON object now."
213	)
214	
215	
216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	# Pure parser (no network) — usable directly for fixture-based tests
218	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	def _strip_code_fences(raw: str) -> str:
221	    """Remove ```json ... ``` / ``` ... ``` fences if the model added them."""
222	    text = raw.strip()
223	    if text.startswith("```"):
224	        # drop first fence line
225	        text = text.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in text else text
226	        # drop trailing fence
227	        if text.rstrip().endswith("```"):
228	            text = text.rstrip()[: -3]
229	    return text.strip()
230	
231	
232	def _extract_json_object(raw: str) -> str:
233	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` object substring in ``raw``.
234	
235	    Falls back to the whole (fence-stripped) string if no braces are found, so
236	    ``json.loads`` raises a clear error the caller can handle.
237	    """
238	    text = _strip_code_fences(raw)
239	    start = text.find("{")
240	    if start == -1:
241	        return text
242	    depth = 0
243	    in_str = False
244	    esc = False
245	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
246	        ch = text[i]
247	        if in_str:
248	            if esc:
249	                esc = False
250	            elif ch == "\\":
251	                esc = True
252	            elif ch == '"':
253	                in_str = False
254	            continue
255	        if ch == '"':
256	            in_str = True
257	        elif ch == "{":
258	            depth += 1
259	        elif ch == "}":
260	            depth -= 1
261	            if depth == 0:
262	                return text[start : i + 1]
263	    return text[start:]
264	
265	
266	def _as_str_list(value: Any) -> list[str]:
267	    """Coerce a JSON value into a clean list of non-empty trimmed strings."""
268	    if value is None:
269	        return []
270	    if isinstance(value, str):
271	        items = [value]
272	    elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
273	        items = list(value)
274	    else:
275	        items = [value]
276	    out: list[str] = []
277	    seen: set[str] = set()
278	    for it in items:
279	        if it is None:
280	            continue
281	        s = str(it).strip()
282	        if not s:
283	            continue
284	        key = s.lower()
285	        if key in seen:
286	            continue
287	        seen.add(key)
288	        out.append(s)
289	    return out
290	
291	
292	def _as_opt_str(value: Any) -> Optional[str]:
293	    """Coerce a scalar JSON value into a trimmed string or None."""
294	    if value is None:
295	        return None
296	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
297	        value = next((v for v in value if v not in (None, "")), None)
298	        if value is None:
299	            return None
300	    s = str(value).strip()
301	    if not s or s.lower() in ("none", "null", "n/a", "na"):
302	        return None
303	    return s
304	
305	
306	def _canon_list(items: list[str], aliases: dict[str, str]) -> list[str]:
307	    """Map each item through ``aliases`` (case/whitespace-insensitive), dedup."""
308	    out: list[str] = []
309	    seen: set[str] = set()
310	    for it in items:
311	        key = re.sub(r"[\s_]+", " ", it.strip().lower())
312	        canon = aliases.get(key, it.strip())
313	        dk = canon.lower()
314	        if dk in seen:
315	            continue
316	        seen.add(dk)
317	        out.append(canon)
318	    return out
319	
320	
321	def _canon_scalar(value: Optional[str], aliases: dict[str, str]) -> Optional[str]:
322	    if value is None:
323	        return None
324	    key = re.sub(r"[\s_]+", " ", value.strip().lower())
325	    return aliases.get(key, value.strip())
326	
327	
328	def parse_constraints_json(raw: str) -> Constraints:
329	    """Parse a raw LLM JSON string into a normalized :class:`Constraints`.
330	
331	    Pure and deterministic — no network. Tolerant of code fences, surrounding
332	    prose, missing keys, and scalar-vs-list mismatches. Unknown keys are
333	    ignored. Raises ``ValueError`` only if no JSON object can be located.
334	    """
335	    obj_text = _extract_json_object(raw)
336	    try:
337	        data = json.loads(obj_text)
338	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
339	        raise ValueError(f"constraint extractor: non-JSON model output: {exc}") from exc
340	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
341	        raise ValueError("constraint extractor: model output was not a JSON object")
342	
343	    c = Constraints(
344	        source_types=_canon_list(
345	            _as_str_list(data.get("source_types")), _SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES
346	        ),
347	        languages=_canon_list(
348	            _as_str_list(data.get("languages")), _LANGUAGE_ALIASES
349	        ),
350	        recency=_as_opt_str(data.get("recency")),
351	        required_coverage=_as_str_list(data.get("required_coverage")),
352	        exclusions=_as_str_list(data.get("exclusions")),
353	        format=_canon_scalar(_as_opt_str(data.get("format")), _FORMAT_ALIASES),
354	        length=_as_opt_str(data.get("length")),
355	        tone=_as_opt_str(data.get("tone")),
356	    )
357	    return c
358	
359	
360	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
361	# Live LLM pass
362	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
363	
364	def _live_enabled() -> bool:
365	    """Whether a live LLM call is permitted for the extractor.
366	
367	    Default OFF for tests/CI so importing/exercising the parser never hits the
368	    network. The live gate follows the repo's env-flag convention (read at call
369	    time so the harness can toggle without re-import). A present
370	    ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` alone is not sufficient — the flag must be truthy —
371	    which keeps unit runs hermetic even on a developer box that has a key.
372	    """
373	    return resolve("PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE").strip().lower() in (
374	        "1",
375	        "true",
376	        "yes",
377	        "on",
378	    )
379	
380	
381	async def extract_constraints_async(
382	    prompt: str,
383	    *,
384	    model: Optional[str] = None,
385	    temperature: float = 0.0,
386	    max_tokens: int = 1024,
387	    client: Any = None,
388	) -> dict[str, Any]:
389	    """Adversarially extract deliverable constraints from ``prompt`` (async).
390	
391	    Reuses the repo LLM gateway (:class:`OpenRouterClient`). Pass ``client`` to
392	    inject a stub/mock in tests (any object with an async ``generate(prompt,
393	    system, max_tokens, temperature)`` returning an object with a ``.content``
394	    string). When ``client`` is None, a real client is constructed only if the
395	    live gate (``PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE``) is enabled; otherwise this raises
396	    ``RuntimeError`` so a test never silently hits the network.
397	
398	    Returns the canonical constraint dict (all keys present).
399	    """
400	    if not isinstance(prompt, str) or not prompt.strip():
401	        # Empty prompt -> empty constraints (no call). Keeps callers simple.
402	        return Constraints().to_dict()
403	
404	    if client is None:
405	        if not _live_enabled():
406	            raise RuntimeError(
407	                "constraint extractor: live LLM disabled. Set "
408	                "PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE=1 to enable a real call, or pass a "
409	                "client= stub in tests."
410	            )
411	        # Lazy import so the OFF/parser path never imports the HTTP client.
412	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
413	            OpenRouterClient,
414	        )
415	
416	        client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
417	
418	    user_prompt = _USER_TEMPLATE.format(prompt=prompt.strip())
419	    response = await client.generate(
420	        prompt=user_prompt,
421	        system=_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
422	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
423	        temperature=temperature,
424	    )
425	    raw = getattr(response, "content", None) or ""
426	    constraints = parse_constraints_json(raw)
427	    return constraints.to_dict()
428	
429	
430	def extract_constraints(
431	    prompt: str,
432	    *,
433	    model: Optional[str] = None,
434	    temperature: float = 0.0,
435	    max_tokens: int = 1024,
436	    client: Any = None,
437	) -> dict[str, Any]:
438	    """Synchronous wrapper around :func:`extract_constraints_async`.
439	
440	    Runs the async extractor to completion. Raises ``RuntimeError`` if called
441	    from within a running event loop (use the async variant there).
442	    """
443	    import asyncio
444	
445	    try:
446	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
447	    except RuntimeError:
448	        pass
449	    else:  # already in a loop
450	        raise RuntimeError(
451	            "extract_constraints() cannot be called from a running event loop; "
452	            "await extract_constraints_async() instead."
453	        )
454	    return asyncio.run(
455	        extract_constraints_async(
456	            prompt,
457	            model=model,
458	            temperature=temperature,
459	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
460	            client=client,
461	        )
462	    )
463

"""Instruction Constraint Extractor.

A general, prompt-agnostic LLM pass that reads a free-text task prompt and
returns the concrete deliverable constraints the author must honor. It is
adversarially prompted to surface BURIED constraints — rules phrased
mid-sentence, tucked into a subordinate clause, or dropped as a soft aside
("ideally...", "where possible...", "and please keep it...") — not just the
obvious headline ask.

Design notes
------------
* Standalone module. Nothing here is wired into the driver or generator; the
  caller decides what (if anything) to do with the returned dict.
* Reuses the repo's existing LLM gateway (``OpenRouterClient.generate``). No
  API keys are hardcoded — the client reads ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` / model
  from the environment exactly as every other module does.
* The live LLM call is split from the pure parser. ``parse_constraints_json``
  turns a raw model string into a normalized :class:`Constraints`, so the shape
  can be unit-tested with a fixture and no network.

Typed fields (all keys always present in the returned dict)
-----------------------------------------------------------
``source_types``     list[str]  e.g. ['journal_article']
``languages``        list[str]  e.g. ['en'] (ISO-639-1 where known)
``recency``          str|None   a cutoff phrase/date, e.g. 'since 2020', or None
``required_coverage``list[str]  topical slots the prompt implies/names
``exclusions``       list[str]  things to exclude (source kinds, topics, ...)
``format``           str|None   e.g. 'literature_review'
``length``           str|None   e.g. '3000 words' / '10 pages', or None
``tone``             str|None   e.g. 'academic', or None
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import os
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Optional
from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Canonical field set + normalization vocab
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# The full, ordered set of keys the extractor guarantees on every result dict.
_FIELDS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "source_types",
    "languages",
    "recency",
    "required_coverage",
    "exclusions",
    "format",
    "length",
    "tone",
)

_LIST_FIELDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
    {"source_types", "languages", "required_coverage", "exclusions"}
)
_SCALAR_FIELDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"recency", "format", "length", "tone"})

# Light, deterministic canonicalization so common phrasings collapse to stable
# tokens the rest of the pipeline can match on. This is intentionally shallow —
# the LLM does the semantic lifting; this only tidies synonyms it emits.
_SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
    "journal article": "journal_article",
    "journal articles": "journal_article",
    "journal-article": "journal_article",
    "journal": "journal_article",
    "peer reviewed journal article": "journal_article",
    "peer-reviewed journal article": "journal_article",
    "peer reviewed article": "peer_reviewed",
    "peer-reviewed": "peer_reviewed",
    "peer reviewed": "peer_reviewed",
    "academic paper": "journal_article",
    "scholarly article": "journal_article",
    "conference paper": "conference_paper",
    "working paper": "working_paper",
    "preprint": "preprint",
    "book": "book",
    "book chapter": "book_chapter",
    "report": "report",
    "government report": "government_report",
    "news article": "news_article",
    "news": "news_article",
    "blog": "blog_post",
    "blog post": "blog_post",
    "website": "website",
    "grey literature": "grey_literature",
    "gray literature": "grey_literature",
}

# ISO-639-1 canonicalization for the languages the prompt is likely to name.
_LANGUAGE_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
    "english": "en",
    "en": "en",
    "eng": "en",
    "english-language": "en",
    "english language": "en",
    "spanish": "es",
    "french": "fr",
    "german": "de",
    "chinese": "zh",
    "mandarin": "zh",
    "japanese": "ja",
    "portuguese": "pt",
    "italian": "it",
    "russian": "ru",
    "arabic": "ar",
    "korean": "ko",
    "dutch": "nl",
}

_FORMAT_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
    "literature review": "literature_review",
    "lit review": "literature_review",
    "systematic review": "systematic_review",
    "scoping review": "scoping_review",
    "narrative review": "narrative_review",
    "meta analysis": "meta_analysis",
    "meta-analysis": "meta_analysis",
    "essay": "essay",
    "report": "report",
    "white paper": "white_paper",
    "policy brief": "policy_brief",
    "briefing": "briefing",
    "summary": "summary",
    "blog post": "blog_post",
    "article": "article",
}


@dataclass
class Constraints:
    """Normalized constraint bundle.

    Every field is populated (lists default empty, scalars default ``None``) so
    downstream code never has to guard for a missing key.
    """

    source_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    recency: Optional[str] = None
    required_coverage: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    format: Optional[str] = None
    length: Optional[str] = None
    tone: Optional[str] = None

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return the canonical dict with all keys present, in field order."""
        return {
            "source_types": list(self.source_types),
            "languages": list(self.languages),
            "recency": self.recency,
            "required_coverage": list(self.required_coverage),
            "exclusions": list(self.exclusions),
            "format": self.format,
            "length": self.length,
            "tone": self.tone,
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prompt (adversarial — hunt buried constraints)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
    "You are a meticulous requirements analyst. Your job is to read a task "
    "prompt written for a report author and extract EVERY constraint the "
    "author must obey to produce an acceptable deliverable.\n\n"
    "Be adversarial about BURIED constraints. Constraints are frequently NOT "
    "stated as a clean bullet list — they hide:\n"
    "  * mid-sentence ('...citing only high-quality, English-language journal "
    "articles.')\n"
    "  * in subordinate clauses ('which should exclude opinion pieces')\n"
    "  * as soft asides ('ideally peer-reviewed', 'where possible since 2020', "
    "'and please keep the tone academic')\n"
    "  * implied by the deliverable type (a 'literature review' implies scholarly "
    "sources and a review structure).\n\n"
    "Extract only what the prompt actually asks for or clearly implies. Do NOT "
    "invent constraints that are not supported by the text. If a field is not "
    "constrained, leave it empty/null — do not guess.\n\n"
    "Return STRICT JSON only (no prose, no code fences) with EXACTLY these keys:\n"
    "  source_types: array of strings — kinds of sources allowed/required "
    "(e.g. 'journal_article', 'peer_reviewed'). If the prompt restricts to a "
    "source kind ('only ... journal articles'), list ONLY that kind.\n"
    "  languages: array of strings — required source languages (prefer ISO-639-1 "
    "like 'en').\n"
    "  recency: string or null — any recency/date-window rule (e.g. 'since 2020', "
    "'last 5 years').\n"
    "  required_coverage: array of strings — specific topics/sub-questions/slots "
    "the review must cover, as named or clearly implied by the prompt.\n"
    "  exclusions: array of strings — anything to exclude (source kinds, topics, "
    "regions).\n"
    "  format: string or null — the deliverable format (e.g. 'literature_review', "
    "'systematic_review', 'report', 'essay').\n"
    "  length: string or null — any length/word/page target.\n"
    "  tone: string or null — required tone/register (e.g. 'academic', 'formal').\n"
)

_USER_TEMPLATE = (
    "Extract the deliverable constraints from the following task prompt.\n"
    "Read it twice: once for the obvious headline ask, and once hunting for "
    "constraints buried mid-sentence or phrased as soft asides.\n\n"
    "TASK PROMPT:\n"
    '"""\n{prompt}\n"""\n\n'
    "Return the strict JSON object now."
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pure parser (no network) — usable directly for fixture-based tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _strip_code_fences(raw: str) -> str:
    """Remove ```json ... ``` / ``` ... ``` fences if the model added them."""
    text = raw.strip()
    if text.startswith("```"):
        # drop first fence line
        text = text.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in text else text
        # drop trailing fence
        if text.rstrip().endswith("```"):
            text = text.rstrip()[: -3]
    return text.strip()


def _extract_json_object(raw: str) -> str:
    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` object substring in ``raw``.

    Falls back to the whole (fence-stripped) string if no braces are found, so
    ``json.loads`` raises a clear error the caller can handle.
    """
    text = _strip_code_fences(raw)
    start = text.find("{")
    if start == -1:
        return text
    depth = 0
    in_str = False
    esc = False
    for i in range(start, len(text)):
        ch = text[i]
        if in_str:
            if esc:
                esc = False
            elif ch == "\\":
                esc = True
            elif ch == '"':
                in_str = False
            continue
        if ch == '"':
            in_str = True
        elif ch == "{":
            depth += 1
        elif ch == "}":
            depth -= 1
            if depth == 0:
                return text[start : i + 1]
    return text[start:]


def _as_str_list(value: Any) -> list[str]:
    """Coerce a JSON value into a clean list of non-empty trimmed strings."""
    if value is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(value, str):
        items = [value]
    elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
        items = list(value)
    else:
        items = [value]
    out: list[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for it in items:
        if it is None:
            continue
        s = str(it).strip()
        if not s:
            continue
        key = s.lower()
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        out.append(s)
    return out


def _as_opt_str(value: Any) -> Optional[str]:
    """Coerce a scalar JSON value into a trimmed string or None."""
    if value is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
        value = next((v for v in value if v not in (None, "")), None)
        if value is None:
            return None
    s = str(value).strip()
    if not s or s.lower() in ("none", "null", "n/a", "na"):
        return None
    return s


def _canon_list(items: list[str], aliases: dict[str, str]) -> list[str]:
    """Map each item through ``aliases`` (case/whitespace-insensitive), dedup."""
    out: list[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for it in items:
        key = re.sub(r"[\s_]+", " ", it.strip().lower())
        canon = aliases.get(key, it.strip())
        dk = canon.lower()
        if dk in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(dk)
        out.append(canon)
    return out


def _canon_scalar(value: Optional[str], aliases: dict[str, str]) -> Optional[str]:
    if value is None:
        return None
    key = re.sub(r"[\s_]+", " ", value.strip().lower())
    return aliases.get(key, value.strip())


def parse_constraints_json(raw: str) -> Constraints:
    """Parse a raw LLM JSON string into a normalized :class:`Constraints`.

    Pure and deterministic — no network. Tolerant of code fences, surrounding
    prose, missing keys, and scalar-vs-list mismatches. Unknown keys are
    ignored. Raises ``ValueError`` only if no JSON object can be located.
    """
    obj_text = _extract_json_object(raw)
    try:
        data = json.loads(obj_text)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
        raise ValueError(f"constraint extractor: non-JSON model output: {exc}") from exc
    if not isinstance(data, dict):
        raise ValueError("constraint extractor: model output was not a JSON object")

    c = Constraints(
        source_types=_canon_list(
            _as_str_list(data.get("source_types")), _SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES
        ),
        languages=_canon_list(
            _as_str_list(data.get("languages")), _LANGUAGE_ALIASES
        ),
        recency=_as_opt_str(data.get("recency")),
        required_coverage=_as_str_list(data.get("required_coverage")),
        exclusions=_as_str_list(data.get("exclusions")),
        format=_canon_scalar(_as_opt_str(data.get("format")), _FORMAT_ALIASES),
        length=_as_opt_str(data.get("length")),
        tone=_as_opt_str(data.get("tone")),
    )
    return c


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Live LLM pass
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _live_enabled() -> bool:
    """Whether a live LLM call is permitted for the extractor.

    Default OFF for tests/CI so importing/exercising the parser never hits the
    network. The live gate follows the repo's env-flag convention (read at call
    time so the harness can toggle without re-import). A present
    ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` alone is not sufficient — the flag must be truthy —
    which keeps unit runs hermetic even on a developer box that has a key.
    """
    return resolve("PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE").strip().lower() in (
        "1",
        "true",
        "yes",
        "on",
    )


async def extract_constraints_async(
    prompt: str,
    *,
    model: Optional[str] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    max_tokens: int = 1024,
    client: Any = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Adversarially extract deliverable constraints from ``prompt`` (async).

    Reuses the repo LLM gateway (:class:`OpenRouterClient`). Pass ``client`` to
    inject a stub/mock in tests (any object with an async ``generate(prompt,
    system, max_tokens, temperature)`` returning an object with a ``.content``
    string). When ``client`` is None, a real client is constructed only if the
    live gate (``PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE``) is enabled; otherwise this raises
    ``RuntimeError`` so a test never silently hits the network.

    Returns the canonical constraint dict (all keys present).
    """
    if not isinstance(prompt, str) or not prompt.strip():
        # Empty prompt -> empty constraints (no call). Keeps callers simple.
        return Constraints().to_dict()

    if client is None:
        if not _live_enabled():
            raise RuntimeError(
                "constraint extractor: live LLM disabled. Set "
                "PG_CONSTRAINT_EXTRACT_LIVE=1 to enable a real call, or pass a "
                "client= stub in tests."
            )
        # Lazy import so the OFF/parser path never imports the HTTP client.
        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            OpenRouterClient,
        )

        client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)

    user_prompt = _USER_TEMPLATE.format(prompt=prompt.strip())
    response = await client.generate(
        prompt=user_prompt,
        system=_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        max_tokens=max_tokens,
        temperature=temperature,
    )
    raw = getattr(response, "content", None) or ""
    constraints = parse_constraints_json(raw)
    return constraints.to_dict()


def extract_constraints(
    prompt: str,
    *,
    model: Optional[str] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    max_tokens: int = 1024,
    client: Any = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Synchronous wrapper around :func:`extract_constraints_async`.

    Runs the async extractor to completion. Raises ``RuntimeError`` if called
    from within a running event loop (use the async variant there).
    """
    import asyncio

    try:
        asyncio.get_running_loop()
    except RuntimeError:
        pass
    else:  # already in a loop
        raise RuntimeError(
            "extract_constraints() cannot be called from a running event loop; "
            "await extract_constraints_async() instead."
        )
    return asyncio.run(
        extract_constraints_async(
            prompt,
            model=model,
            temperature=temperature,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            client=client,
        )
    )

content.content:
1	"""I-scope-001 — the generic SCOPE + TIMELINE enforcement plan builder.
2	
3	Generalizes the PROVEN date-window WEIGHT seam (``evidence_selector`` ~2881: build a per-URL
4	demote map + an out-of-window tail-partition set + disclosed exclusion records) to arbitrary
5	SCOPE facets driven by the ontology. It emits, for one corpus + one protocol:
6	
7	  * ``url_to_scope_weight``   — multiplicative (0,1] demote for WEIGHT-demoted sources
8	                               (mirrors ``url_to_date_weight``); folded into the SAME
9	                               selection sort key. WEIGHT path — never a drop.
10	  * ``out_of_scope_urls``     — the tail-partition set (mirrors ``_oow_urls``).
11	  * ``grounding_excluded_ids``— HARD-mode urls masked OUT of the billed grounding set
12	                               ``evidence_for_gen`` (restrict-to / exclude-hard / hard
13	                               timeline). KEPT in the pool + disclosure, never deleted.
14	  * ``must_include_urls``     — op='include' boost/pin flag (additive, non-demoting).
15	  * ``scope_excluded_records``— PRISMA-style telemetry, one row per decision.
16	
17	DNA §-1.3: WEIGHT-DON'T-FILTER + DISCLOSE. A HARD mask removes a source from the ANSWER
18	GROUNDING surface only, never from the evidence pool / strict_verify / NLI / D8 / provenance
19	(the ONE hard gate stays byte-untouched). Fail-open: an unresolved facet => neutral (weight
20	1.0, never punished). Default-OFF ``PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE`` => empty plan => byte-
21	identical selection + no grounding mask. All knobs config-driven (LAW VI).
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import logging
26	import os
27	import re
28	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
29	from typing import Any, Mapping
30	
31	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_facet_classifier import (
32	    classify_source_facets,
33	    facet_default_weight,
34	    load_scope_ontology,
35	)
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.constraint_enforcement")
38	
39	_ENFORCE_FLAG = "PG_SCOPE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCE"
40	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
41	
42	_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"\b(19|20|21)\d{2}\b")
43	_ISO_YM_RE = re.compile(r"\b((?:19|20|21)\d{2})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])\b")
44	
45	
46	def scope_enforcement_enabled() -> bool:
47	    """I-scope-001 enforcement kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF (operator activates on the slate).
48	    OFF => ``build_scope_enforcement`` returns an EMPTY plan => byte-identical selection."""
49	    return os.getenv(_ENFORCE_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
50	
51	
52	@dataclass
53	class ScopeEnforcementPlan:
54	    """The demote / mask / boost decisions for one corpus under one protocol's scope+timeline
55	    intent. Empty plan => byte-identical widest+deepest run."""
56	
57	    url_to_scope_weight: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
58	    out_of_scope_urls: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
59	    grounding_excluded_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
60	    must_include_urls: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
61	    scope_excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
62	    scope_disclosed_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
63	
64	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
65	        return not (
66	            self.url_to_scope_weight
67	            or self.out_of_scope_urls
68	            or self.grounding_excluded_ids
69	            or self.must_include_urls
70	        )
71	
72	
73	def _field(source: "Any", *names: str) -> "Any":
74	    if isinstance(source, Mapping):
75	        for n in names:
76	            if n in source and source.get(n) is not None:
77	                return source.get(n)
78	        return None
79	    for n in names:
80	        v = getattr(source, n, None)
81	        if v is not None:
82	            return v
83	    return None
84	
85	
86	def _row_url(row: "Any") -> str:
87	    return str(_field(row, "source_url", "url") or "")
88	
89	
90	def _row_pub_ym(row: "Any") -> "tuple[int, int | None] | None":
91	    """Best-effort (year, month|None) from a row's date fields. None when undated
92	    (fail-open — an undated row is NEVER excluded by a timeline window).
93	
94	    Month-precision keys (``pub_date`` is the production month field emitted by
95	    live_retriever) are read BEFORE the year-only fallbacks (``publication_year`` /
96	    ``year``) so month precision wins: a live row carrying ``pub_date``="2023-05"
97	    plus a year-only ``publication_year``=2023 resolves to (2023, 5), not (2023, None).
98	    Reading only the year would degrade every real row to YEAR precision and wrongly
99	    hard-mask an in-window month source under a HARD month-precision cutoff (§-1.3)."""
100	    for key in ("pub_date", "publication_date", "published", "date", "publication_year", "year"):
101	        v = _field(row, key)
102	        if v is None:
103	            continue
104	        s = str(v)
105	        m = _ISO_YM_RE.search(s)
106	        if m:
107	            return (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)))
108	        y = _YEAR_RE.search(s)
109	        if y:
110	            return (int(y.group(0)), None)
111	    return None
112	
113	
114	def _parse_window_bound(iso: "str | None") -> "tuple[int, int | None] | None":
115	    if not iso:
116	        return None
117	    s = str(iso)
118	    m = _ISO_YM_RE.search(s)
119	    if m:
120	        return (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)))
121	    y = _YEAR_RE.search(s)
122	    if y:
123	        return (int(y.group(0)), None)
124	    return None
125	
126	
127	def _ym_index(ym: "tuple[int, int | None]", *, ceil: bool) -> int:
128	    """Month index (year*12+month). A year-only bound uses month 1 for a floor and 12 for a
129	    ceiling so a whole-year window is inclusive."""
130	    year, month = ym
131	    if month is None:
132	        month = 12 if ceil else 1
133	    return year * 12 + month
134	
135	
136	def _row_out_of_window(
137	    row: "Any", start_idx: "int | None", end_idx: "int | None"
138	) -> bool:
139	    """True iff the row has an EXPLICIT publication date outside [start, end]. Undated =>
140	    False (fail-open, kept)."""
141	    pub = _row_pub_ym(row)
142	    if pub is None:
143	        return False
144	    idx = _ym_index((pub[0], pub[1]), ceil=False)
145	    if start_idx is not None and idx < start_idx:
146	        return True
147	    if end_idx is not None:
148	        # compare at the row's own precision against the ceiling
149	        row_ceil = _ym_index((pub[0], pub[1]), ceil=(pub[1] is None))
150	        if row_ceil > end_idx:
151	            return True
152	    return False
153	
154	
155	def _facet_ids(facets: list[dict[str, Any]], op: str, strictness: "str | None") -> list[str]:
156	    out = []
157	    for f in facets:
158	        if not isinstance(f, dict):
159	            continue
160	        if str(f.get("op")) != op:
161	            continue
162	        if strictness is not None and str(f.get("strictness")) != strictness:
163	            continue
164	        fid = str(f.get("facet_id") or "")
165	        if fid:
166	            out.append(fid)
167	    return out
168	
169	
170	def _named_match(label: str, identity: dict[str, Any], row: "Any") -> bool:
171	    """Match a named-include source to a row by its org acronym / label tokens against the
172	    row url / host / title. Deterministic, conservative (acronym is case-sensitive)."""
173	    hay = " ".join(
174	        str(_field(row, k) or "") for k in ("source_url", "url", "title")
175	    )
176	    acronym = str(identity.get("acronym") or "").strip()
177	    if acronym and re.search(r"\b" + re.escape(acronym) + r"\b", hay):
178	        return True
179	    # fall back to a distinctive lowercase token from the label (>= 4 chars)
180	    for tok in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]{4,}", label):
181	        if tok.lower() in ("guidelines", "guideline", "reports", "report", "data",
182	                           "publications", "publication", "standards", "framework"):
183	            continue
184	        if tok.lower() in hay.lower():
185	            return True
186	    return False
187	
188	
189	def build_scope_enforcement(
190	    protocol: "dict[str, Any] | None",
191	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
192	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
193	) -> ScopeEnforcementPlan:
194	    """Build the scope+timeline enforcement plan for one corpus (§2.2).
195	
196	    Reads ``protocol['scope_constraints']`` (from ``ScopeConstraints.to_dict``) + the timeline
197	    strictness inside ``protocol['user_constraints']`` + the date window from
198	    ``protocol['date_range']``. Classifies each row via ``classify_source_facets`` and applies
199	    the per-(op,strictness) rule. Returns an EMPTY plan when the enforce flag is OFF or when no
200	    scope/timeline constraint was stated (byte-identical). Pure; never raises."""
201	    plan = ScopeEnforcementPlan()
202	    if not scope_enforcement_enabled():
203	        return plan
204	    if not protocol:
205	        return plan
206	    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
207	    if not rows:
208	        return plan
209	
210	    ont = ontology if ontology is not None else load_scope_ontology()
211	    sc = protocol.get("scope_constraints") or {}
212	    uc = protocol.get("user_constraints") or {}
213	    facets = list(sc.get("facets") or []) if isinstance(sc, dict) else []
214	    named_include = list(sc.get("named_include") or []) if isinstance(sc, dict) else []
215	
216	    prefer_weight = _facet_ids(facets, "prefer", "weight")
217	    prefer_hard = _facet_ids(facets, "prefer", "hard")
218	    include_ids = _facet_ids(facets, "include", None)
219	    exclude_weight = _facet_ids(facets, "exclude", "weight")
220	    exclude_hard = _facet_ids(facets, "exclude", "hard")
221	
222	    # trigger spans for disclosure
223	    span_by_facet = {
224	        str(f.get("facet_id")): str(f.get("trigger_span") or "")
225	        for f in facets if isinstance(f, dict)
226	    }
227	
228	    # timeline (hard) window
229	    timeline_strictness = str(uc.get("timeline_strictness") or "weight")
230	    dr = protocol.get("date_range") or {}
231	    if isinstance(dr, (list, tuple)):
232	        _s_iso, _e_iso = (dr[0] if len(dr) > 0 else None), (dr[1] if len(dr) > 1 else None)
233	    elif isinstance(dr, dict):
234	        _s_iso, _e_iso = dr.get("start"), dr.get("end")
235	    else:
236	        _s_iso, _e_iso = None, None
237	    _start = _parse_window_bound(_s_iso)
238	    _end = _parse_window_bound(_e_iso)
239	    _start_idx = _ym_index(_start, ceil=False) if _start else None
240	    _end_idx = _ym_index(_end, ceil=(_end[1] is None)) if _end else None
241	    timeline_hard_active = (
242	        timeline_strictness == "hard" and (_start_idx is not None or _end_idx is not None)
243	    )
244	
245	    have_scope = bool(
246	        prefer_weight or prefer_hard or include_ids or exclude_weight or exclude_hard
247	        or named_include
248	    )
249	    if not have_scope and not timeline_hard_active:
250	        return plan
251	
252	    def _min_weight(ids: list[str]) -> float:
253	        ws = [facet_default_weight(i, ont) for i in ids]
254	        return min(ws) if ws else facet_default_weight("", ont)
255	
256	    for row in rows:
257	        url = _row_url(row)
258	        if not url:
259	            continue
260	        try:
261	            row_facets, _basis = classify_source_facets(row, ont)
262	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - fail-open: an unresolved row is neutral
263	            row_facets = set()
264	
265	        # op='include' — additive boost; non-matching NOT demoted.
266	        matched_include = [i for i in include_ids if i in row_facets]
267	        for named in named_include:
268	            if isinstance(named, dict) and _named_match(
269	                str(named.get("label") or ""), dict(named.get("identity") or {}), row
270	            ):
271	                plan.must_include_urls.add(url)
272	                plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
273	                    "source_url": url, "matched_facet": str(named.get("label") or ""),
274	                    "op": "include", "action": "named_include_boost", "weight": 1.0,
275	                    "reason": "named-include pinned", "trigger_span": str(named.get("label") or ""),
276	                })
277	        if matched_include:
278	            plan.must_include_urls.add(url)
279	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
280	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(matched_include),
281	                "op": "include", "action": "include_boost", "weight": 1.0,
282	                "reason": "in requested include facet",
283	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(matched_include[0], ""),
284	            })
285	
286	        # HARD exclusion — masked from grounding, KEPT in pool + disclosure.
287	        hard_excluded = False
288	        matched_exclude_hard = [i for i in exclude_hard if i in row_facets]
289	        if matched_exclude_hard:
290	            hard_excluded = True
291	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
292	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
293	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(matched_exclude_hard),
294	                "op": "exclude", "action": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_disclosed",
295	                "weight": 0.0, "reason": "in do-not-use facet (hard)",
296	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(matched_exclude_hard[0], ""),
297	            })
298	        elif prefer_hard and not any(i in row_facets for i in prefer_hard):
299	            hard_excluded = True
300	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
301	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
302	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": ",".join(prefer_hard),
303	                "op": "prefer", "action": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_disclosed",
304	                "weight": 0.0, "reason": "outside restrict-to facet (hard)",
305	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(prefer_hard[0], ""),
306	            })
307	
308	        # HARD timeline — out-of-window masked from grounding, KEPT + disclosed.
309	        if timeline_hard_active and _row_out_of_window(row, _start_idx, _end_idx):
310	            hard_excluded = True
311	            plan.grounding_excluded_ids.add(url)
312	            _pub = _row_pub_ym(row)
313	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
314	                "source_url": url,
315	                "matched_facet": "timeline",
316	                "op": "timeline", "action": "timeline_hard_excluded_disclosed",
317	                "weight": 0.0,
318	                "reason": "outside hard timeline window",
319	                "pub_ym": (f"{_pub[0]:04d}-{_pub[1]:02d}" if _pub and _pub[1]
320	                           else (str(_pub[0]) if _pub else None)),
321	                "trigger_span": str(uc.get("timeline_trigger_span") or ""),
322	            })
323	
324	        if hard_excluded:
325	            plan.scope_disclosed_rows.append({
326	                "source_url": url,
327	                "disclosed": "hard_excluded_from_grounding_kept_in_corpus",
328	            })
329	            continue
330	
331	        # WEIGHT demote (prefer-weight miss OR exclude-weight hit). Multiplicative, kept.
332	        w = 1.0
333	        demote_reason = ""
334	        demote_facet = ""
335	        if prefer_weight and not any(i in row_facets for i in prefer_weight):
336	            w = min(w, _min_weight(prefer_weight))
337	            demote_reason = "outside preferred facet"
338	            demote_facet = ",".join(prefer_weight)
339	        matched_exclude_weight = [i for i in exclude_weight if i in row_facets]
340	        if matched_exclude_weight:
341	            w = min(w, _min_weight(matched_exclude_weight))
342	            demote_reason = "in de-emphasized facet"
343	            demote_facet = ",".join(matched_exclude_weight)
344	        # P1 fix (§2.2/§2.4 include != prefer): a source the user explicitly INCLUDED
345	        # (op='include' / named-include) is NEVER also demoted — include is an ADDITIVE
346	        # boost, so it must not sink its own pinned source. The include block above ran
347	        # first this iteration, so ``must_include_urls`` already carries this url when it
348	        # was included. Skipping the demote keeps the disclosure honest (a source is never
349	        # recorded as both boosted and demoted) and matches the selector's include-pin.
350	        if 0.0 < w < 1.0 and url not in plan.must_include_urls:
351	            plan.url_to_scope_weight[url] = w
352	            plan.out_of_scope_urls.add(url)
353	            plan.scope_excluded_records.append({
354	                "source_url": url, "matched_facet": demote_facet,
355	                "op": ("exclude" if matched_exclude_weight else "prefer"),
356	                "action": "demoted_out_of_scope_kept", "weight": w,
357	                "reason": demote_reason,
358	                "trigger_span": span_by_facet.get(
359	                    (demote_facet.split(",")[0] if demote_facet else ""), ""
360	                ),
361	            })
362	
363	    if not plan.is_empty():
364	        logger.info(
365	            "[scope_enforce] I-scope-001: demoted=%d hard_masked=%d boosted=%d "
366	            "(timeline_hard=%s)",
367	            len(plan.url_to_scope_weight), len(plan.grounding_excluded_ids),
368	            len(plan.must_include_urls), timeline_hard_active,
369	        )
370	    return plan
371

1066	    raw = resolve('PG_SELECT_RECENCY_EPSILON').strip()

1074	
1075	def _row_year(row: dict[str, Any]) -> int | None:
1076	    """Publication year from `row['year']` or `row['metadata']['year']`.
1077	    Returns None if absent / non-numeric / outside [1900, 2100]. No network."""
1078	    val = row.get("year")
1079	    if val is None:
1080	        meta = row.get("metadata")
1081	        if isinstance(meta, dict):
1082	            val = meta.get("year")
1083	    if val is None:
1084	        return None
1085	    try:
1086	        year = int(val)
1087	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1088	        return None
1089	    if year < _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR or year > _RECENCY_MAX_YEAR:
1090	        return None
1091	    return year
1092	
1093	
1094	def _relevance_band(score: float, epsilon: float) -> float:
1095	    """Bucket a relevance score into a band. Higher score → higher band.
1096	    epsilon <= 0 → exact score (only exact ties share a band)."""
1097	    if epsilon <= 0:
1098	        return score
1099	    return math.floor(score / epsilon)
1100	
1101	
1102	def _year_sort_value(row: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
1103	    """Ascending-sort value for 'newer first': negated year; a missing year
1104	    maps below any real year so undated rows sort LAST within a band (but are
1105	    never excluded)."""
1106	    year = _row_year(row)
1107	    return -(year if year is not None else _RECENCY_MIN_YEAR - 1)
1108	
1109	
1110	def _relevance_recency_key(
1111	    item: tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]],
1112	    enabled: bool,
1113	    epsilon: float,
1114	) -> tuple:
1115	    """Sort fragment ranking by relevance then (soft) recency, or by raw
1116	    relevance when disabled. Callers append the original index for full
1117	    determinism. Disabled → `(-score,)`, byte-identical to the prior key."""
1118	    _idx, score, _tier, row = item
1119	    if not enabled:
1120	        return (-score,)
1121	    # Within a relevance band, newer year first; then EXACT score as the
1122	    # sub-tiebreaker. Because the band is monotonic in score and exact score
1123	    # breaks within-band ties, an all-undated corpus reproduces the prior
1124	    # pure-(-score) order EXACTLY — recency only reorders DATED same-band rows.
1125	    return (-_relevance_band(score, epsilon), _year_sort_value(row), -score)
1126	
1127	
1128	def _recency_telemetry_note(
1129	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1130	    epsilon: float,
1131	) -> str:
1132	    """Note emitted ONLY when the recency tiebreaker is enabled."""
1133	    dated = sum(1 for s in scored if _row_year(s[3]) is not None)
1134	    return (
1135	        f"recency_tiebreak enabled epsilon={epsilon} "
1136	        f"dated={dated}/{len(scored)}"
1137	    )
1138	
1139	
1140	# ── #956 (S2, 2026-05-30): source-diversity passes ──────────────────────────
1141	# Tier quota != topical diversity: 20 T1 RCTs all on ONE sub-topic satisfy the
1142	# T1 quota while starving the other sub-topics, tanking per-sub-topic coverage.
1143	# Two SOFT post-selection passes operate ONLY on post-floor slack and ALWAYS
1144	# preserve tier minimums (Codex brief-gate P2). Because the selected set sits
1145	# exactly at the tier quotas, every quota-preserving swap is SAME-TIER: a
1146	# diversity swap replaces a same-tier NON-reserved relevance-fill row with a
1147	# same-tier pool row, so no tier ever drops below quota and no floor-reserved
1148	# (M-42e/M-51/M-42c/M-42d) or sub-query-reserved row is ever evicted. Both
1149	# passes run on the truncating main path only (short-pool keeps every row, so
1150	# diversity is already maximal there). Codex rulings: k=1, cap_frac=0.5,
1151	# reservation BEFORE domain cap.
1152	_SUBQUERY_K_DEFAULT = 1
1153	_DOMAIN_CAP_FRAC_DEFAULT = 0.5
1154	
1155	
1156	def _env_flag_on(name: str) -> bool:
1157	    return os.environ.get(name, "1").strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
1158	
1159	
1160	def _subquery_reserve_config() -> tuple[bool, int]:
1161	    enabled = _env_flag_on("PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_RESERVE")
1162	    raw = resolve('PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_K').strip()
1163	    try:
1164	        k = int(raw) if raw else _SUBQUERY_K_DEFAULT
1165	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1166	        k = _SUBQUERY_K_DEFAULT
1167	    return enabled, max(1, k)
1168	
1169	
1170	def _domain_cap_config() -> tuple[bool, float]:
1171	    enabled = _env_flag_on("PG_SELECT_DOMAIN_CAP")
1172	    raw = resolve('PG_SELECT_DOMAIN_CAP_FRAC').strip()
1173	    try:
1174	        frac = float(raw) if raw else _DOMAIN_CAP_FRAC_DEFAULT
1175	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1176	        frac = _DOMAIN_CAP_FRAC_DEFAULT
1177	    return enabled, frac
1178	
1179	
1180	# ── I-perm-003 (#1197): corpus-size-scaled evidence-selection budget ─────────
1181	# The generator-facing cap is a FIXED `max_rows` (PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN, default
1182	# 20 — #1070/#1078). At 1000-URL retrieval the fixed cap truncates the pool to a
1183	# constant slice regardless of how much best-ranked evidence the corpus actually
1184	# holds. This PREVENTATIVE knob scales the selection budget WITH the pool size so
1185	# a larger corpus feeds proportionally more BEST-ranked rows (the existing
1186	# tier-balanced + relevance/recency truncation already picks best-ranked, never
1187	# first-N — this only raises the budget it operates under).
1188	#
1189	# HONEST SCOPE: on the beatboth8 corpus the selector drops 0 sources (the ~90%
1190	# loss is UPSTREAM extraction, owned by I-perm-007), so this changes nothing
1191	# there. It is a forward guard for when the upstream pool is genuinely large.
1192	#
1193	# DEFAULT OFF. When `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE` is unset/0/false/no/off the helper
1194	# returns `base_max_rows` UNCHANGED and `select_evidence_for_generation` is
1195	# byte-identical to the prior behaviour (no reassignment, no telemetry).
1196	#
1197	# FLOOR semantics: effective = max(base_max_rows, ceil(pool_size * frac)),
1198	# optionally clamped to a ceiling. The `max(...)` guarantees scaling NEVER drops
1199	# the budget below the operator/code cap (a small pool with a low frac keeps the
1200	# existing cap — never a regression).
1201	_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT = 0.30
1202	# 0 = no ceiling (scale unbounded with the pool). A positive value clamps the
1203	# scaled budget so an enormous pool can't blow past an operator-set ceiling.
1204	_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT = 0
1205	
1206	
1207	def _selection_scale_enabled() -> bool:
1208	    """Flag `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE` (default OFF). ON only on explicit
1209	    truthy ('1'/'true'/'yes'/'on'). OFF → byte-identical prior selection AND
1210	    no scaling telemetry. Inverse default of `_env_flag_on` ON-by-default."""
1211	    raw = resolve('PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE').strip().lower()
1212	    return raw in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1213	
1214	
1215	def _selection_scale_frac() -> float:
1216	    """Budget-per-pool-row fraction `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC`
1217	    (default 0.30). Non-positive / unparseable → default (FAIL SOFT to a sane
1218	    positive fraction; a 0 frac would make scaling a no-op via the floor)."""
1219	    raw = resolve('PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC').strip()
1220	    if not raw:
1221	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1222	    try:
1223	        frac = float(raw)
1224	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1225	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1226	    # Reject non-finite (inf/nan): a positive `inf` parses but would OverflowError in
1227	    # `math.ceil(pool_size * frac)` (Codex iter-1 P2). FAIL SOFT to the sane default.
1228	    return frac if (frac > 0 and math.isfinite(frac)) else _SELECTION_SCALE_FRAC_DEFAULT
1229	
1230	
1231	def _selection_scale_ceiling() -> int:
1232	    """Optional absolute ceiling `PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING`
1233	    (default 0 = no ceiling). Clamps the scaled budget so an enormous pool can't
1234	    overshoot an operator cap. Values <= 0 / unparseable → no ceiling."""
1235	    raw = resolve('PG_SWEEP_SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING').strip()
1236	    if not raw:
1237	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1238	    try:
1239	        ceiling = int(raw)
1240	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1241	        return _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1242	    return ceiling if ceiling > 0 else _SELECTION_SCALE_CEILING_DEFAULT
1243	
1244	
1245	def _scaled_max_rows(pool_size: int, base_max_rows: int) -> tuple[int, str | None]:
1246	    """Corpus-size-scaled selection budget (I-perm-003, default OFF).
1247	
1248	    Returns ``(effective_max_rows, note)``. When the flag is OFF, returns
1249	    ``(base_max_rows, None)`` — the caller MUST treat that as a byte-identical
1250	    no-op (no telemetry note appended). When ON, returns the FLOOR-guarded scaled
1251	    budget ``max(base_max_rows, ceil(pool_size * frac))`` (optionally clamped to a
1252	    ceiling) plus a single telemetry string for the selection notes.
1253	    """
1254	    if not _selection_scale_enabled():
1255	        return base_max_rows, None
1256	    frac = _selection_scale_frac()
1257	    ceiling = _selection_scale_ceiling()
1258	    scaled = math.ceil(pool_size * frac)
1259	    effective = max(base_max_rows, scaled)
1260	    clamped = False
1261	    if ceiling > 0 and effective > ceiling:
1262	        # Never below the base cap even when the ceiling is < base (floor wins).
1263	        effective = max(base_max_rows, ceiling)
1264	        clamped = True
1265	    note = (
1266	        f"selection_scale pool={pool_size} frac={frac} "
1267	        f"base_max_rows={base_max_rows} scaled={scaled} "
1268	        f"effective={effective} ceiling={ceiling or 'none'}"
1269	        f"{' clamped' if clamped else ''}"
1270	    )
1271	    return effective, note
1272	
1273	
1274	def _row_query_origin(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1275	    """The sub-query that surfaced the row (`query_origin`), or `_unlabeled`."""
1276	    return str(row.get("query_origin") or "") or "_unlabeled"
1277	
1278	
1279	def _row_domain(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
1280	    """Registrable-ish domain (last two host labels) for the per-domain cap."""
1281	    url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
1282	    if not url:
1283	        return ""
1284	    try:
1285	        from urllib.parse import urlparse
1286	        host = (urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"http://{url}").hostname or "").lower()
1287	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
1288	        return ""
1289	    parts = host.split(".")
1290	    return ".".join(parts[-2:]) if len(parts) >= 2 else host
1291	
1292	
1293	def _priority_sort_key(
1294	    item: tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]],
1295	    rec_enabled: bool,
1296	    rec_eps: float,
1297	) -> tuple:
1298	    """Within-tier priority for diversity swap decisions — same ordering the
1299	    selector uses (banded relevance + #955 recency, then original index).
1300	    Ascending = best first; max(...) of this key = worst (lowest priority)."""
1301	    return (*_relevance_recency_key(item, rec_enabled, rec_eps), item[0])
1302	
1303	
1304	def _reserve_subqueries(
1305	    selected: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1306	    scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
1307	    protected_ids: set[int],
1308	    k: int,
1309	    rec_enabled: bool,
1310	    rec_eps: float,
1311	) -> tuple[int, set[int]]:
1312	    """Same-tier swap so each sub-query origin present in a tier's pool reaches
1313	    >= k selected rows in that tier, evicting only NON-protected slack rows whose
1314	    origin is OVER-represented (count > k). Returns (swaps, brought_in_ids)."""
1315	    selected_ids = {id(it) for it in selected}
1316	    pool_by_tier: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
1317	    for it in scored:
1318	        if id(it) not in selected_ids:
1319	            pool_by_tier[it[2]].append(it)
1320	    brought_in: set[int] = set()
1321	    swaps = 0
1322	    for tier in {it[2] for it in selected}:
1323	        sel_pos = [i for i, it in enumerate(selected) if it[2] == tier]
1324	        origin_count = Counter(_row_query_origin(selected[i][3]) for i in sel_pos)
1325	        cand_by_origin: dict[str, list] = defaultdict(list)
1326	        for it in pool_by_tier.get(tier, []):
1327	            cand_by_origin[_row_query_origin(it[3])].append(it)
1328	        missing = sorted(
1329	            [o for o in cand_by_origin if origin_count.get(o, 0) < k],
1330	            key=lambda o: (-max(c[1] for c in cand_by_origin[o]), o),
1331	        )
1332	        for origin in missing:
1333	            cands = sorted(
1334	                cand_by_origin[origin],
1335	                key=lambda c: _priority_sort_key(c, rec_enabled, rec_eps),
1336	            )
1337	            ci = 0
1338	            while origin_count.get(origin, 0) < k and ci < len(cands):
1339	                evictable = [
1340	                    i for i in sel_pos
1341	                    if id(selected[i]) not in protected_ids
1342	                    and id(selected[i]) not in brought_in
1343	                    and origin_count[_row_query_origin(selected[i][3])] > k
1344	                    and _row_query_origin(selected[i][3]) != origin
1345	                ]
1346	                if not evictable:
1347	                    break
1348	                evict_i = max(
1349	                    evictable,
1350	                    key=lambda i: _priority_sort_key(selected[i], rec_enabled, rec_eps),
1351	                )
1352	                bring = cands[ci]
1353	                ci += 1
1354	                old_origin = _row_query_origin(selected[evict_i][3])
1355	                selected[evict_i] = bring
1356	                brought_in.add(id(bring))
1357	                origin_count[old_origin] -= 1
1358	                origin_count[origin] = origin_count.get(origin, 0) + 1
1359	                swaps += 1
1360	    return swaps, brought_in
1361	
1362	
1363	# ── I-perm-023 (#1215): diversity-aware selection (constrained-greedy) ────────
1364	# A default-OFF forward guard that diversifies the SELECTED set on the coverage
1365	# axes the existing floor stack does NOT already cover. Entity custody is owned by
1366	# the M-42e/M-51 anchor-primary floors, mechanism by the M-42c floor, the 1-per
1367	# present-jurisdiction reservation by M-41d — so this pass adds SAFETY-CATEGORY +
1368	# EVIDENCE-CLASS coverage (plus jurisdiction diversity BEYOND the M-41d 1-per floor)
1369	# via the SAME post-floor same-tier swap mechanism as the #956 passes. It NEVER
1370	# touches a floor / quota / protected row, so floor parity is guaranteed BY
1371	# CONSTRUCTION. Swaps are COVERAGE-MONOTONE: a swap fires only when the incoming row
1372	# adds a NOVEL bucket AND the evicted row's every bucket stays covered by another
1373	# selected row, so distinct coverage can only INCREASE, never drop an axis.
1374	# Faithfulness (Codex design-gate iter-2): selection only changes the generator's
1375	# candidate menu; the effective generator evidence_pool starts from selected_rows
1376	# (and may add sanctioned prepends + M-52 live pulls), and strict_verify / the
1377	# 4-role evaluator / D8 re-check every sentence against the cited span unchanged —
1378	# so a swap can at worst trade one verifiable row for another, NEVER admit
1379	# unsupported prose. No-op at drb_76 scale (pool<=cap returns via the short-pool
1380	# branch before this region runs).
1381	
1382	# Safety-category taxonomy (FDA/DailyMed SPL labeling sections). Keyword -> category,
1383	# preference-only (a miss costs diversity, never faithfulness). Versioned constant.
1384	_GREEDY_SAFETY_CATEGORIES: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
1385	    ("boxed_warning", ("boxed warning", "black box warning", "black-box warning")),
1386	    ("contraindication", ("contraindication", "contraindicated")),
1387	    ("warning_precaution", ("warnings and precautions", "warning", "precaution")),
1388	    ("adverse_reaction", ("adverse reaction", "adverse event", "adverse effect",
1389	                          "side effect")),
1390	    ("drug_interaction", ("drug interaction", "drug-drug interaction")),
1391	    ("pregnancy_lactation", ("pregnancy", "lactation", "breastfeeding", "teratogen")),
1392	    ("hepatic_renal", ("hepatotoxicity", "hepatic impairment", "renal impairment",
1393	                       "nephrotox")),
1394	    ("hypoglycemia", ("hypoglycemia", "hypoglycaemia")),
1395	    ("overdose", ("overdose", "overdosage")),
1396	)
1397	
1398	# Evidence-class taxonomy. Keyword -> class, preference-only. Versioned constant.
1399	_GREEDY_EVIDENCE_CLASSES: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
1400	    ("meta_analysis", ("meta-analysis", "meta analysis", "systematic review",
1401	                       "cochrane")),
1402	    ("rct", ("randomized", "randomised", "double-blind", "placebo-controlled",
1403	             "phase 3 trial", "phase iii")),
1404	    ("guideline", ("guideline", "consensus statement", "recommendation",
1405	                   "position statement")),
1406	    ("real_world", ("real-world", "observational", "registry", "cohort study",
1407	                    "case-control", "post-marketing")),
1408	    ("regulatory", ("prescribing information", "package insert",
1409	                    "summary of product characteristics", "smpc")),
1410	)
1411	
1412	_GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS_DEFAULT = 24
1413	_GREEDY_AXES_DEFAULT = ("safety", "class", "jurisdiction")
1414	_GREEDY_VALID_AXES = frozenset(_GREEDY_AXES_DEFAULT)
1415

1417	def _greedy_match_category(
1418	    row: dict[str, Any], taxonomy: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...],
1419	) -> str | None:
1420	    """First taxonomy category whose keyword appears in the row's title/statement/
1421	    quote (case-insensitive substring), in taxonomy order. None if no match."""
1422	    combined = " ".join((
1423	        _row_title_text(row),
1424	        str(row.get("statement") or ""),
1425	        str(row.get("direct_quote") or ""),
1426	    )).lower()
1427	    for category, tokens in taxonomy:
1428	        if any(tok in combined for tok in tokens):
1429	            return category
1430	    return None
1431	
1432	
1433	def _greedy_active_axes() -> tuple[str, ...]:
1434	    """Call-time active axis set (env override PG_GREEDY_AXES, default safety/class/
1435	    jurisdiction). Unknown axis names are ignored; empty/invalid -> the default."""
1436	    raw = resolve('PG_GREEDY_AXES').strip()
1437	    if not raw:
1438	        return _GREEDY_AXES_DEFAULT
1439	    axes = tuple(a for a in (x.strip() for x in raw.split(",")) if a in _GREEDY_VALID_AXES)
1440	    return axes or _GREEDY_AXES_DEFAULT
1441	
1442	
1443	def _row_coverage_buckets(
1444	    row: dict[str, Any], axes: tuple[str, ...],
1445	) -> frozenset[tuple[str, str]]:
1446	    """The (axis, value) coverage buckets a row belongs to across the ACTIVE axes.
1447	    safety_category / evidence_class are keyword-derived; jurisdiction reuses the
1448	    existing M-41d host predicate. A row with no axis value contributes nothing."""
1449	    out: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
1450	    if "safety" in axes:
1451	        s = _greedy_match_category(row, _GREEDY_SAFETY_CATEGORIES)
1452	        if s:
1453	            out.add(("safety", s))
1454	    if "class" in axes:
1455	        c = _greedy_match_category(row, _GREEDY_EVIDENCE_CLASSES)
1456	        if c:
1457	            out.add(("class", c))
1458	    if "jurisdiction" in axes:
1459	        j = _row_jurisdiction(row)
1460	        if j:
1461	            out.add(("jurisdiction", j))
1462	    return frozenset(out)
1463	
1464	
1465	def _constrained_greedy_config() -> tuple[bool, int]:
1466	    """Call-time config for the I-perm-023 diversity pass. DEFAULT OFF (NOT
1467	    `_env_flag_on`, which defaults ON — Codex design-gate iter-2 P2.3) -> when unset
1468	    the pass never runs and selected/notes/to_dict stay byte-identical. Returns
1469	    (enabled, max_swaps)."""
1470	    raw = resolve('PG_SELECT_CONSTRAINED_GREEDY').strip().lower()
1471	    enabled = raw in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
1472	    raw_n = resolve('PG_GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS').strip()
1473	    try:
1474	        n = int(raw_n) if raw_n else _GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS_DEFAULT
1475	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1476	        n = _GREEDY_MAX_SWAPS_DEFAULT
1477	    return enabled, max(0, n)
